name: Band Delay
overview: Delays are followed by user selectable modulated filters.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Delay Mix
parameters.description: Relative level of Delay A and B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the relative level of Delay A and Delay B. Delay Mix’s mixing behavior depends on whether you’re using mono or stereo outputs. For mono output: Delay Mix = 0%, output 1 will have only delay A’s contribution. Delay Mix = 50%, output 1 has an equal amount of delay A and delay B. Delay Mix = 100%, output 1 will have only delay B’s contribution. For stereo output: Delay Mix = 0%, both outputs will have only delay A’s contribution. Delay Mix = 50%, delay A goes to output 1 only and delay B goes to output 2 only. Delay Mix = 100%, both outputs will have only Delay B’s contribution.

parameters.name: Delay A
parameters.fullDescription: Sets delay time from 0 to 3000 ms (milliseconds). With Tempo Sync OFF, delay is displayed in ms. With Tempo Sync ON, delay can be sync’d to the tempo and is displayed as a rhythmic sub-division of the tempo beat value from No Delay to a whole note in common note increments.

parameters.name: Delay B
parameters.fullDescription: Same as A.

parameters.name: Feedback A
parameters.fullDescription: Controls level of Feedback A, the number of repeats.

parameters.name: Feedback B
parameters.fullDescription: Same as A.

parameters.name: Resonance
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the resonance or sharpness of the filter. Varies from 0 (subtle effects) to 10 (dramatic resonance effects).

parameters.name: Modulation Depth
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the amount that the filter cut-off or center frequencies are modulated/shifted.

parameters.name: Modulation Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the modulation rate for the filter center frequencies (0 to 5 Hz).

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: Low Pass / Band Pass / High Pass selection
parameters.fullDescription: Select filter type – Low Pass, Band Pass or Hi Pass.

performanceParams.name: Repeat
performanceParams.description: A momentary or latching parameter that sets the feedback to the highest value.

name: Bouquet Delay
overview: Emulation of analog delay pedals with two different flavors and modern tricks.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: wet/dry mixer, 0 to 50 mixes in the delayed signal, 50 to 100 mixes out the dry signal.

parameters.name: Delay
parameters.fullDescription: Delay length control for the Delay mode in milliseconds. Shorter delay lengths brighten the signal delay line output. Both LoFi and Modern modes can make use of the full delay time range, but the hardware that served as the inspiration for LoFi mode was only intended to be used up to 300 ms. Anything above that and you’re seriously under-clocking the chip.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the level of feedback in the delay path for both the Short and Long delay modes. Capable of driving the delay into self oscillation close to 10 (not precise due to other settings in the BBD affecting feedback) in Modern mode, and at varying amounts (depending on delay time settings) in LoFi. (75 ms to 1000 ms)

parameters.name: Mod Depth
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of modulation applied to the BBD delay line. Note that modulation amounts will vary depending on the Delay time.

parameters.name: LFO Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the speed/rate of the modulation LFO. (0.01 Hz to 20 Hz)

parameters.name: LFO Shape
parameters.fullDescription: LFO Shape of the modulation oscillator. The choices are: SINE FATSINE TRIANGLE SQUARE SAW RAMP NOISE

parameters.name: Delay Mode
parameters.fullDescription: (Lofi/Modern) Two different options for delay sounds. LoFi emulates an early bucket brigade pedal that was limited to a delay time of 300 ms. Modern will be a little brighter/cleaner sounding and the self-oscillation will be a little more uniform.

parameters.name: Jump Interval
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the ratio used to jump the delay time when using the PitchJump performance parameter. (m3, M3, P4, P5, Oct.)

parameters.name: Stereo Tap Division
parameters.fullDescription: When stereo outputs are connected, a second tap out of the BBD emulation is connected to the second channel. This controls the point at which this second delay is tapped out of the BBD.

performanceParams.name: Self Osc
performanceParams.description: A momentary or latching performance parameter that causes the BBD emulation to self-oscillate.

performanceParams.name: Pitch Jump
performanceParams.description: A momentary or latching performance parameter that instantaneously scales the delay time (clock frequency) of the BBD emulation by the ratio determined by the Jump Interval parameter. This causes a momentary pitch shift of any sound being fed back through the BBD emulation.

performanceParams.name: Retrigger
performanceParams.description: Retriggers the modulation LFO to the beginning of the cycle. Useful for re-syncing during playback, or creative effects.

parameters.name: Ducking Ratio
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the ducking ratio or the degree to which the delay is attenuated.

parameters.name: Threshold
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the ducking threshold - the audio amplitude - at which ducking kicks in (-36 dB to -66 dB).

parameters.name: Release Time
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the release time from 500 to 10 ms. With the release time set to short values, the delay will kick in quickly when you stop playing. With the release time set to longer values, the delay will stay ducked for a while. Longer release times are useful when you’re playing a riff and don’t want the delay to kick in between notes.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.fullDescription: A low pass/high pass filter variable from -100 (max high pass) to 0 (no filtering) to 100 (max low pass) to change the tone of your delay repeats.

parameters.name: Modulation Wave Shape

parameters.name: Modulation Depth

parameters.fullDescription: Controls the mix of dry/filtered signal input to ping-pong delay.

name: Head Space
overview: Authentic tape delay emulation with adjustable playback heads, wow/flutter, drive, and hiss controls.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the levels of the dry and wet (tape delayed) signals to the output of the effect. 0 to 5 mixes in the delayed signals. 5 to 10 mixes out the dry signal.

parameters.name: Delay Time
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the longest delay length possible of any one Head by changing the tape speed. Use a Head Div of 1 to reach full delay time of any one tap. 200-3000 ms.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Runs the tape emulation at either Half or Full speed while keeping the overall delay amounts the same. At Full speed the machine ranges from 30 ips to 1.875 ips. At Half the machine ranges from 15 ips to a glacially slow speed of 0.9375 ips.

parameters.name: Rec Drive
parameters.fullDescription: Higher levels saturate the tape creating an overdriven sound

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Master Feedback control for all Heads. Capable of driving the tape delay into self oscillation from around 7 to 10 (not precise due to other settings affecting feedback structure and levels in the feedback loop).

parameters.name: Fdbk Path
parameters.fullDescription: Controls which Heads contribute their output to the feedback loop. The options are: 1, 2, 3, or 4 1 & 2 1 & 3 1 & 4 2 & 3 2 & 4 3 & 4 1 & 2 & 3 1 & 2 & 4 1 & 3 & 4 2 & 3 & 4 All

parameters.name: Wow & Flutter
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of mechanical anomalies of a real tape machine. These anomalies manifest as pitch warbles and delay variations. Use 0 for a perfectly constructed and maintained high end tape machine. Use 10 for a lower end, old tape machine in need of some serious repair.

parameters.name: Tape Hiss
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of tape hiss. 0 is no hiss. 10 is the most amount of hiss. Control is global over all playback heads.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: EQ control for all head outputs
parameters.fullDescription: Equalization control for all Head outputs. The outputs of the EQs are in the feedback loop. -10 to 0 Low cut shelf. 0 to 10 Hi cut filter (low pass). The following parameters apply to all 4 tape heads:

parameters.name: Boil Time
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the time it takes for the Boil performance parameter to ramp up to its apex. 200 to 10000 ms.

parameters.name: Break Time
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the time it takes for the machine to ramp down to its slowest speed when the Breakdown performance parameter is engaged. 200 to 10000 ms.

performanceParams.name: Boil
performanceParams.description: These reels sure do seem like they’re spinning fast. Does anyone smell smoke?

performanceParams.name: Breakdown
performanceParams.description: Someone pulled the plug, the servos have decided they’re tired of spinning. Either way things really seem to be s l o w i n g d o w …

parameters.name: Saturation
parameters.fullDescription: Simulates analog tape saturation. Ranges from ‘0’ (none) to ‘10’ (max) for the warm compression and distortion associated with overdriven tape.

parameters.name: Tape Wow
parameters.fullDescription: Simulates analog tape Wow. Wow is a term used to describe relatively slowly changing pitch and amplitude modulations caused by problems with the motor or tape transport that causes the tape’s motion across the head to vary. A well maintained tape recorder should have no audible Wow. Ranges from ‘0’ (none) to ‘10’ (max).

parameters.fullDescription: Simulates tape machine Flutter. Like Wow, Flutter is caused when the tape motion across the magnetic heads isn’t constant. Flutter is a more rapidly changing variation than Wow. Flutter ranges from 0 (none) to 10 (max).

parameters.name: Filter

parameters.name: Length
parameters.fullDescription: Total time over which the Taps are spaced in, up to 10 seconds.

parameters.name: Taps
parameters.fullDescription: The number of delay taps, from 1 to 64.

parameters.name: Predelay
parameters.fullDescription: The amount of time before the first Tap starts, up to 1 second.

parameters.name: Spread
parameters.fullDescription: The rhythmic spacing of the taps. Positive values will group more taps towards the end for a “speeding-up” delay sound. Negative values will group taps towards the beginning, for a “slowing-down” feeling. Zero results in constant spacing.

parameters.name: Taper
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the fade of the taps. Positive values for a fade-down over the taps. Negative values for fade-up over the taps. Zero will result in equal gain across all taps.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Repeats of a length-valued delay that is fed back around the entire multi-tap machine.

parameters.name: Tone
parameters.fullDescription: A tone control. Positive values for brighter sounding taps. Negative values for darker sounding taps.

parameters.name: Slurm
parameters.fullDescription: Juicy tap slurring/smearing and modulation.

parameters.name: Chop

parameters.name: Width
parameters.fullDescription: Adjust how the taps are evenly distributed in the stereo field. 0 will result in the no distribution, while positive values will increasingly distribute the taps more, and negative values will reverse this image.

name: Aggravate
overview: An Octave fuzz inspired by famous classic analog octave fuzzes from the 70's with versatile controls and chewy tone.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: The clean/dirty mix, full left is clean, full right is dirty.

parameters.name: Fuzz
parameters.fullDescription: The amount of fuzz.

parameters.name: Octave
parameters.fullDescription: Enables or disables the analog-style octave effect

parameters.name: Thick
parameters.fullDescription: Makes the fuzz thick (or thicker).

parameters.name: Mids
parameters.fullDescription: Midrange control for the stage. -100 to 0 removes midrange for a more scooped sound. 0 to 100 adds midrange.

parameters.name: Tone
parameters.fullDescription: -100 to 0 rolls off the treble creating a bassier tone. 0 to 100 brightens the effect.

parameters.name: Bias
parameters.fullDescription: Increasing this control will add a “spitty” characteristic to your tone.

parameters.name: Filter Env
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the sensitivity of the filter envelope follower. Negative values will start at a high cutoff frequency and move lower as you play; positive values will start at a low cutoff frequency and move higher as you play. At 0 there will be no filtering.

parameters.name: Env Shape
parameters.fullDescription: Shape of the enveloper filter. Exponential is suggested for guitar, while Linear is suggested for bass.

parameters.name: Resonance
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the resonance of the filter.

parameters.name: Pump Depth
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the ratio of the pumping compressor. Increasing this will make the pumping effect more pronounced.

parameters.name: Pump Control
parameters.fullDescription: Sets what the pump is controlled by. Pump Rate uses the included LFO, manual lets you control the Pump as a performance control.

parameters.name: Pump Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the speed of the pump rate from .50 - 5 Hz. Higher pump rates work better with shorter release times.

parameters.name: Release
parameters.fullDescription: Release time of the pump effect.

performanceParams.name: Pump
performanceParams.description: Manually triggers the Pump effect. Only works when Pump Control is set to Manual.

performanceParams.name: Retrig
performanceParams.description: Retriggers the LFO of Pump Rate.

parameters.fullDescription: The clean/dirty mix, full left is clean, full right is dirty.

parameters.name: Drive
parameters.fullDescription: The overdrive amount. Ranges from subtle boost/overdrive to full on distortion with Grit and Sustain controls pushing it into fuzz territory.

parameters.name: Compressor/Sustainer
parameters.fullDescription: Compression/Sustain which is Pre distortion (counterclockwise), or Post distortion (clockwise). The sustainer is specially designed to vary the numerous parameters of a typical compressor such as the ratio, attack, release, and the makeup gain to keep the overall loudness consistent.

parameters.name: Sag
parameters.description: Creates sputtery, crushed, and broken sounds
parameters.fullDescription: Turn it up to get increasingly sputtery, crushed, and broken sounds. Inspired by power rail sag of poorly designed tube amps and the dead and dying gear of times past.

parameters.name: Octaves
parameters.fullDescription: Mixes in/out lower and higher pitch-shifted octaves before the distortion and compression.

parameters.name: Grit
parameters.fullDescription: Adds more low end before the distortion for a gritty chugging sound.

parameters.name: Bass
parameters.fullDescription: Cut and boost lower frequencies to hollow out the sound or add some thud.

parameters.name: Mids
parameters.fullDescription: Cut and boost mid range frequencies (frequency selectable with Mids Frequency control) to scoop some muddiness or punch through a mix.

parameters.name: Mids Frequency
parameters.description: Tunable center frequency for mids control
parameters.fullDescription: Tunable center frequency of the Mids cut/boost. Similar to a parked wah when boosted up high. Smoothly changes when connected to an expression pedal.

parameters.name: Treble
parameters.fullDescription: Cut and Boost higher frequencies to mellow out the sound or emphasize higher harmonics.

name: PitchFuzz
overview: Multi-effect combining Fuzz, three Pitch Shifters, and two Delays with flexible routing for arpeggiated effects.

parameters.name: Fuzz
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of Fuzz/Distortion generated after the input signal. A setting of 0 completely bypasses the Fuzz effect. Use 1 to 50 for a distortion type effect and 51 to 100 for more of a Fuzz type effect.

parameters.name: Fuzz Tone
parameters.fullDescription: Tone shaper for the fuzz effect.

parameters.name: Pitch Amount
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the level of the three Pitch Shifters. From 0 to 3 Voices. Pitch A: 0 to 1.0, Pitch A + B: 1.0 to 2.0, Pitch A + B + C: 2.0 to 3.0.

parameters.name: Pitch A
parameters.fullDescription: Adjust the pitch shift amount of the A voice. Range is +/- 2 octaves with micro pitch shift ability at unison (+/- 25c).

parameters.name: Pitch B
parameters.fullDescription: Same as Pitch A.

parameters.name: Pitch C
parameters.fullDescription: Same as Pitch A.

parameters.name: Delay Level
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of both Delays in the signal path as well as two types of Delay routings (Group Delay and Arp Delay). Starting fully counter-clockwise, Group Delay sends the whole signal including all of the Pitched Voices to both delays. Turning past center activates Arpeggiated Delay Mode. In this mode, only voices B and C are fed to the delays (separately and respectively) allowing the creation of arpeggiated effects. Try this with Pitch Amount set to 3.0.

parameters.name: Delay A
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the Delay time for Delay A from 0 to 2500 ms when Tempo Sync is OFF. With Tempo Sync ON, Delay is adjusted in note division increments from No Delay to a Whole Note in the most common note divisions.

parameters.name: Delay B
parameters.fullDescription: Same as Delay A.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the amount of feedback for both delays and contains two feedback types (F1 and F2). F1 links both delay times to create a rhythmic, repeating pattern where the longer delay sets the pattern length. The shorter delay will not repeat again until the longer delay has passed. F2 is a traditional feedback control, where delay times are independent.

name: Sculpt
overview: Multi-band Distortion with Envelope Follower Control Filters for carving out your own sound.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: The clean/dirty mix, all the way left is clean, all the way right is dirty.

parameters.name: Band Mix
parameters.description: Low to high band blend ratio
parameters.fullDescription: The mix between the low and high band.

parameters.name: Crossover Frequency
parameters.fullDescription: The crossover frequency where the low band and high band are split.

parameters.name: Low Drive
parameters.fullDescription: Overdrive of the low band.

parameters.name: High Drive
parameters.fullDescription: Overdrive of the high band.

parameters.name: Compressor
parameters.fullDescription: Pre distortion (counter-clockwise), or Post distortion (clockwise). Turn counter-clockwise to juice up the harmonics in the distortion, or turn clockwise for some sparkly compressor spank. The Sculpt compressor is specially designed to vary the numerous parameters of a typical compressor such as the ratio, attack, release and the makeup gain to keep the overall loudness consistent.

parameters.name: Low Boost
parameters.fullDescription: Boosts the low end either Pre distortion (counter-clockwise) for chuggier low end, or Post distortion (clockwise) for smoother low end.

parameters.name: Filter-Pre
parameters.fullDescription: Peaking filter Pre distortion. Turning left sweeps a cutting filter up in frequency. Turning right sweeps a boosting filter up in frequency, similar to having a parked wah before the distortion. Smoothly changes when connected to an expression pedal.

parameters.name: Filter-Post
parameters.fullDescription: Peaking filter Post distortion. Turning left sweeps a cutting filter up in frequency. Turning right sweeps a boosting filter up in frequency, similar to having a parked wah after the distortion. Smoothly changes when connected to an expression pedal.

parameters.name: Envelope Follower
parameters.description: Modulates both filters based on input
parameters.fullDescription: Envelope follower that modulates both Filter-Pre and Filter-Post according to this input sensitivity setting. The values of Filter-Pre and Filter-Post become the depths that the envelope glides up to. Interesting dynamic changes are achieved when pre and post are set to opposite sweeps, e.g. Pre boost, and Post cut, etc.

parameters.name: Stereo Mode
parameters.fullDescription: Split features unique spectral panning effects that spread the hi and lo bands out into the separate channels. Dual Mono outputs the same signal to both outputs channels.

name: WeedWacker
overview: Two stage, serial overdrive effect reminiscent of a famous green overdrive.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Global mix level of the entire WeedWacker effect.

parameters.name: Gate Threshold
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the noise gate threshold for the entire effect. -90 dB is off.

parameters.name: Stage 2
parameters.fullDescription: Activates or bypasses the 2nd overdrive stage. The following parameters are available for both Stage 1 and 2:

parameters.name: Drive 1
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the distortion level of the overdrive stage. 0 is cleanest. 100 is dirtiest.

parameters.name: Tone 1
parameters.fullDescription: Tone control for the stage. -100 to 0 rolls off the treble creating a bassier tone. 0 to 100 brightens the effect.

parameters.name: Mids 1
parameters.fullDescription: Midrange control for the stage. -100 to 0 removes midrange for a more scooped sound. 0 to 100 adds midrange.

parameters.name: Level 1
parameters.fullDescription: Final output level control for the stage.

parameters.name: Drive 2
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the distortion level of the overdrive stage. 0 is cleanest. 100 is dirtiest.

parameters.name: Tone 2
parameters.fullDescription: Tone control for the stage. -100 to 0 rolls off the treble creating a bassier tone. 0 to 100 brightens the effect.

parameters.name: Mids 2
parameters.fullDescription: Midrange control for the stage. -100 to 0 removes midrange for a more scooped sound. 0 to 100 adds midrange.

parameters.name: Level 2
parameters.fullDescription: Final output level control for the stage.

name: EQ Compressor
overview: A multi-featured parametric equalizer coupled with a dynamic, intuitive compressor for premium tone shaping.

parameters.name: Gain 1
parameters.fullDescription: The gain of the first parametric filter. Provides 12 dB of boost or 18 dB of attenuation.

parameters.name: Frequency 1
parameters.fullDescription: The center frequency of the first parametric filter. The frequency ranges from 30 Hz to 1500 Hz.

parameters.name: Width 1
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the bandwidth of the 1st parametric filter. A value of 10 represents a larger bandwidth while a value of 1 represents a narrower bandwidth.

parameters.name: Gain 2
parameters.fullDescription: The gain for the 2nd parametric filter. Provides 12 dB of boost or 18 dB of attenuation.

parameters.name: Frequency 2
parameters.fullDescription: The center frequency of the first parametric band. The frequency ranges from 1000 Hz to 9999 Hz.

parameters.name: Width 2
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the bandwidth of the 2nd parametric filter. A value of 10 represents a larger bandwidth while a value of 1 represents a narrower bandwidth.

parameters.name: Bass
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the gain on the Low Frequency Shelving Filter which is centered around 400 Hz with a slope of 8 dB/Octave. You can boost the lows by 12 dB or cut by 18 dB.

parameters.name: Treble
parameters.fullDescription: The gain on the High Frequency Shelving Filter which is centered around 1800 Hz with a slope of 8 dB/Octave. You can boost the highs by 12 dB or cut by 18 dB.

parameters.name: Compressor
parameters.fullDescription: The amount of compression applied to the signal. The values to the left half of the knob will affect the Pre-EQ compression, increasing the amount of compression as you move it counterclockwise. The values to the right half of the knob will affect the Post-EQ compression, increasing the amount of compression as you move it clockwise. The compressor is specially designed to vary the numerous parameters of a typical compressor such as the ratio, attack, release and the makeup gain to keep the overall loudness consistent.

parameters.name: Trim
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the level at the output of the signal path. Provides 12 dB of boost or 12 dB of attenuation. The Algorithm is designed to “gracefully” clip if there is too much gain inside the EQ.

name: Cosmic Web
overview: High-end rack processing with a modern, macro granular package blending reverse pitched grains with classic Eventide reverb.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Dry / Wet Mixer.

parameters.name: Reverb
parameters.fullDescription: Post Mix reverb on the whole signal.

parameters.name: AutoRetrig
parameters.fullDescription: Level for dynamic retriggering of Reverse grain start AND gating effect.

parameters.name: Level
parameters.fullDescription: Level for Voice A/B.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.fullDescription: Length of Voice A/B grain in msecs or note values (tempo sync).

parameters.name: Pitch
parameters.description: Voice A/B pitch, controls grain speed
parameters.fullDescription: Pitch of voice A/B. Note: it’s really a speed control too, a higher pitch will repeat the grain within the Length.

parameters.name: Detune
parameters.description: Fine pitch detuning with stereo spreading
parameters.fullDescription: Fine pitch detuning of Voice A/B with a multi voice stereo spreading effect.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Amount grain delay feedback for Voice A/B.

parameters.name: Crystals
parameters.fullDescription: Crystals style feedback around voice structures. Choices are: Off, A, B, A+B.

parameters.name: Ping Pong
parameters.fullDescription: Puts Voices in ping pong mode using the stereo voices in series. Reverse Direction will have initial reverse grain on the left, and forward on the right. Choices are: Off, A, B, A+B.

parameters.name: Reverse
parameters.fullDescription: Reverse grain playback. Choices are: Off, A, B, A+B.

performanceParams.name: Retrigger
performanceParams.description: Manual reverse grain and gating retrigger for creative rhythmic effects.

performanceParams.name: Repeat A
performanceParams.description: Sets infinite delay feedback for voice A, sustaining the current grain pattern indefinitely.

performanceParams.name: Repeat B
performanceParams.description: Sets infinite delay feedback for voice B, sustaining the current grain pattern indefinitely.

performanceParams.name: Freeze
performanceParams.description: Freezes the reverb tail, sustaining it indefinitely without new input.

performanceParams.name: Dump
performanceParams.description: Clears the memory of both voices, silencing any sustained grains.

name: Glitch
overview: Slice, distort, and reconstruct audio with three distinct glitch types that work independently or stack together.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/Dry Mixer.

parameters.name: Interrupt
parameters.fullDescription: If set to On, Glitches will pause (interrupt) the dry audio.

parameters.name: Glitch Type
parameters.fullDescription: Sets which glitch flavors are active. If a combination is selected, all active glitch flavors will be heard whenever a glitch occurs. Burst: This is the familiar behavior of many short grains grouped together and pitch shifted. Alias: This will apply aliasing to the input signal if no other glitch is happening, or else it will apply aliasing to the output of another glitch (Burst or Clock). Clock: If a Burst glitch is occurring, then the clock changes will be applied to the burst. Otherwise, a grain equal to the LENGTH will be triggered and the Clock jump will occur during this grain.

parameters.name: Trigger Mode
parameters.fullDescription: Chance: glitches occur pseudorandomly according to the value of Chance. Envelope: a glitch occurs when the input reaches the Env Thresh value. Manual: glitches only occur when the Glitch Perform Parameter is pressed.

parameters.name: Chance
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the probability (chance) that a glitch occurs when Trigger Mode is set to Chance.

parameters.name: Env Thresh
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the threshold for input audio to trigger a glitch when Trigger Mode is set to Env.

parameters.name: Glitch Time
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the length of a glitch. 60 ms - 8000 ms, or tempo synced

parameters.name: Glitch Fade
parameters.fullDescription: A symmetric fade in/out for the Glitches when they occur as a percentage of the Glitch Time. 0 to 100% of Glitch Time

parameters.name: Flux
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the number of times per Length that Alias Amount, Burst Rate, and the Clock will be randomly modulated. For Alias Amount and Burst Rate, the random values will be less than or equal to what the user has set for these values (e.g. if Alias Amount is 50, all random Alias Amount values will be less than or equal to 50). The clock may jump to any frequency. In tempo mode, the random modulations will be evenly distributed over the length; otherwise, they are randomly distributed over the length.

parameters.name: Alias Amount
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the amount of aliasing applied by decreasing the sample rate of the aliaser as the control is turned up. At a value of 100, the aliaser downsamples the input to 1.5 KHz.

parameters.name: Burst Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the rate at which grains are triggered during a Burst Glitch. Higher values correspond to more grains triggered during a burst, resulting in a denser sound.

parameters.name: Burst Pitch
parameters.fullDescription: If set to a single interval, then when a Burst Glitch occurs, the audio will be pitch shifted by this interval whenever a pitch jump occurs. If Random Down, then -Oct, -P5, and -P4 will be chosen randomly. If Random Up, then +P4, +P5, and +Oct will be chosen randomly. If Random All, then any interval will be chosen randomly, including unison. Note that pitches closer to the most recent pitch will be favored for the next randomly selected pitch.

parameters.name: Clock Target
parameters.fullDescription: When FLUX is OFF, this sets the frequency that a clock glitch will resample the buffer to. The clock values are 8, 9, 12, 16, 18, 24, 32, or 36 KHz (Note that these values from right to left represent shifting by -P4, -P5, -Oct, -P11, -P12, -2Oct, -P18, -P19)

parameters.name: Reverb Mix
parameters.fullDescription: A wet/dry control for the Reverb. Note that reverb is added to the dry signal as well.

parameters.name: Reverb Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the decay time of the reverb.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the width of the stereo image.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.fullDescription: A resonant filter post-grains, pre-reverb. From 0 to 100: Decreases the cutoff frequency of a resonant low pass filter. From 0 to -100: Increases the cutoff frequency of a resonant high pass filter.

performanceParams.name: Glitch
performanceParams.description: Triggers continuous glitch looping that persists until disengaged.

performanceParams.name: Glitch M
performanceParams.description: Momentary glitch that is active only while the footswitch is held.

name: GrainMod
overview: Morph your sound with modulated grain parameters. Three independent LFOs target Trigger Rate, Grain Duration, and Filter.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/Dry mixer.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.fullDescription: Increases the width of the stereo image. 0 is mono (though the reverb at the end of the signal chain is still stereo).

parameters.name: Delay
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the amount of delay used by the granular processor. 0 to 1000 milliseconds, or tempo synced

parameters.name: Retrig Source
parameters.fullDescription: Determines what control will retrigger the LFOs. If Manual, the perform mode footswitch RETRIGGER control will be used. If Env, the LFOs are retriggered every time the input is above a fixed, low threshold. This is to create a more predictable modulation sound whenever a new note/chord is played.

parameters.name: Reverb Mix
parameters.fullDescription: A one knob reverb (from Cosmic Web) that increases mix and decay as the knob is increased. Note that reverb is added to the dry signal as well.

parameters.name: Direction Mod
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of square wave modulation to the grain direction. At 0 all grains will be played forward, and at 100 all grains will be played backward. For intermediate values, grains will be played in both directions, with more grains being played in reverse as the knob is increased.

parameters.name: Direction Mod Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the rate of the Direction LFO. 0.01 to 20 Hz, or tempo synced.

parameters.name: Trigger Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the rate at which new grains are triggered. 0.2 to 32 Hz, or tempo synced.

parameters.name: Grain Duration
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the duration of each grain. 20 to 2000 ms, or tempo synced.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.fullDescription: A resonant filter post-grains, pre-reverb. From 0 to 100: Decreases the cutoff frequency of a resonant low pass filter. From 0 to -100: Increases the cutoff frequency of a resonant high pass filter. Trigger, Grain Duration, and Filter each have a dedicated LFO with the following parameters:

performanceParams.name: Retrigger
performanceParams.description: Resets all LFO waveform cycles to their starting position.

name: Stutter
overview: The classic granular effect. Create burst patterns with precise control over timing and subdivision.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/Dry mixer.

parameters.name: Interrupt
parameters.fullDescription: If set to On, stutter burst will pause (interrupt) the dry audio.

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.fullDescription: A resonant filter post-grains, pre-reverb. From 0 to 100: Decreases the cutoff frequency of a resonant low pass filter. From 0 to -100: Increases the cutoff frequency of a resonant high pass filter.

parameters.name: Trigger Mode
parameters.fullDescription: Chance: Stutter bursts occur pseudorandomly according to the value of Chance. Envelope: A stutter burst occurs when the input reaches the Env Thresh value. Manual: Stutter bursts only occur when the Stutter Perform Parameter is pressed.

parameters.name: Chance
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the probability (chance) of a stutter burst when Trigger Mode is set to Chance.

parameters.name: Env Thresh
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the threshold for input audio to trigger a stutter burst when Trigger Mode is set to Env.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the length of a stutter burst.

parameters.name: Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the spacing of each individual stutter in a stutter burst. Also determines the rate at which audio is panned if using the Width control.

parameters.name: Smear
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the duration of the individual stutters within a burst as a percentage of Rate. 25% to 300% of Rate.

parameters.name: Pitch Chance
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the probability of a pitch jump by the interval or combination set by the Pitch control.

parameters.name: Pitch
parameters.fullDescription: If set to a single interval, then the audio will be pitch shifted by this interval whenever a pitch jump occurs. If Random Down, then -Oct, -P5, and -P4 will be chosen randomly. If Random Up, then +P4, +P5, and +Oct will be chosen randomly. If Random All, then any interval will be chosen randomly, including unison. Note that pitches closer to the most recent pitch will be favored for the next randomly selected pitch.

parameters.name: Detune
parameters.fullDescription: Applies detuning to the stuttered output. Detuning will still be applied to unison shifted audio.

parameters.name: Spread Type
parameters.fullDescription: Speed: As Spread Amt is increased, stutters will be grouped increasingly closer together over a stutter burst. Slow: As Spread Amt is increased, stutters will be spaced increasingly farther apart over a stutter burst. Double+Half: As Spread Amt is increased, the probability of Stutter Rate either being halved or doubled is increased. At 100, you will only hear stutter bursts that are randomly half or double speed compared to the Stutter Time setting.

parameters.name: Spread Amount
parameters.fullDescription: Increases the audible effect for each setting in the Spread Type parameter.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.fullDescription: Increases the stereo image of the stutter bursts. 0 is mono (though the reverb at the end of the signal chain is still stereo).

parameters.name: Reverb Mix
parameters.fullDescription: A wet/dry control for the Reverb. Note that reverb is added to the dry signal as well.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the decay time of the reverb.

performanceParams.name: Stutter
performanceParams.description: Triggers continuous stutter looping that persists until disengaged.

performanceParams.name: Stutter M
performanceParams.description: Momentary stutter that is active only while the footswitch is held.

performanceParams.name: Retrigger
performanceParams.description: Resets the LFO waveform to its starting position.

name: Crystals
overview: Combines twin reverse pitch shifters, delays, feedback, and reverb to generate cascading pitched delays and granular textures.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Pitch A/B Mix
parameters.description: Ratio between Pitch A and B levels
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the ratio of the level of Pitch A to Pitch B.

parameters.name: Pitch Shift A
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of pitch shift for A in cents (1 cent = 1/100th of a semitone).

parameters.name: Pitch Shift B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of pitch shift for B in cents (1 cent = 1/100th of a semitone).

parameters.name: Reverse Delay Buffer A
parameters.description: Reverse time buffer length, ms or tempo-synced
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the length of the reverse time buffer for A. With Tempo Sync OFF, delay is displayed in ms. With Tempo Sync ON, delay can be sync’d to the tempo and is displayed as a rhythmic sub-division of the tempo beat value.

parameters.name: Reverse Delay Buffer B
parameters.description: Reverse time buffer length, ms or tempo-synced
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the length of the reverse time buffer for B. With Tempo OFF, delay is displayed in ms. With Tempo ON, delay can be sync’d to the tempo and is displayed as a rhythmic sub-division of the tempo beat value.

parameters.name: Reverb Mix Level
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the Wet/dry mix of the reverb level, where 100 is an all wet signal. Note that this mix level is part of the wet signal path, so the global Mix control will still affect the amount of wet signal being heard overall.

parameters.name: Reverb Decay Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the Reverb Decay rate.

parameters.name: Feedback A
parameters.fullDescription: Controls level of Feedback A.

parameters.name: Feedback B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls level of Feedback B.

performanceParams.name: Flex
performanceParams.description: Shifts both voices up one octave.

name: Diatonic
overview: Pitch shifters track notes and shift by harmonic interval based on Key and Scale. Twin independent pitch changers with delays and feedback.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Pitch A/B Mix
parameters.description: Ratio of Pitch A to B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the ratio of the level of Pitch A to Pitch B. Note: The A/B mix is set before the feedback delays so that feedback can continue on A or B and not be affected by new audio when the Pitch Mix control is turned completely to the opposite channel. This allows you to create a mini 'looper' effect.

parameters.name: Pitch Shift A
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the harmonic interval (pitch shift) for Pitch A.

parameters.name: Pitch Shift B
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the harmonic interval (pitch shift) for Pitch B.

parameters.name: Delay A
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of time delay of the A pitch shifted output. With Tempo Sync OFF, delay is displayed in ms. With Tempo Sync ON, delay can be sync’d to the tempo and is displayed as a rhythmic sub-division of the tempo beat value.

parameters.name: Delay B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of time delay of the B pitch shifted output. With Tempo OFF, delay is displayed in ms. With Tempo ON, delay can be sync’d to the tempo and is displayed as a rhythmic sub-division of the tempo beat value.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the key.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the scale. The supported scales are: Major Minor Dorian Phrygian Lydian Mixolydian Locrian Harmonic Minor Melodic Minor Whole Tone Enigmatic Neapolitan Hungarian

parameters.name: Feedback A
parameters.fullDescription: Controls level of voice A Feedback. The feedback delay length is the length of either Delay A or Delay B, whichever is longer, to make sure both voices fade out simultaneously.

parameters.name: Feedback B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls level of voice B Feedback. The feedback delay length is the length of either Delay A or Delay B, whichever is longer, to make sure both voices fade out simultaneously.

parameters.name: Quantization
parameters.fullDescription: Quantizes the notes that are not in the selected key scale to fit within the selected key and scale.

performanceParams.name: Learn
performanceParams.description: Press-and-hold the Learn switch while playing a note and the H90 will set the key to that note.

name: H910 H949
overview: Emulates legendary Eventide H910 Harmonizer, the world's first real-time pro-audio pitch changer.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Pitch A/B Mix
parameters.description: Ratio between Pitch A and B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the ratio of the level of Pitch A to Pitch B.

parameters.name: Pitch Shift Up A
parameters.description: Pitch shift ratio for voice A
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of pitch shift for voice A expressed as a ratio.

parameters.name: Pitch Shift Down B
parameters.description: Pitch shift ratio for voice B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of pitch shift for voice B expressed as a ratio.

parameters.name: Delay A
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of time delay of the A pitch-shifted output. With Tempo Sync OFF, delay is displayed in ms. With Tempo Sync ON, delay can be sync’d to the tempo and is displayed as a rhythmic sub-division of the tempo beat value.

parameters.name: Delay B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of time delay of the B pitch-shifted output. With Tempo Sync OFF, delay is displayed in ms. With Tempo Sync ON, delay can be sync’d to the tempo and is displayed as a rhythmic sub-division of the tempo beat value.

parameters.name: Splice Type
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the type of Harmonizer emulated: H910 (just a tiny bit unstable, flickering display, free-running oscillators add randomness), H949-1 (may cause glitches with increasing frequency as pitch ratio deviates from 1:1, more appropriate for smaller pitch ratios), H949-2 (intelligent splicing reduces glitching but adds coloration, suitable for extreme pitch ratios), Modern (takes advantage of powerful DSP to further reduce glitching).

parameters.name: Pitch Coarse/Fine Control
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the type of pitch ratio control for Pitch A and Pitch B parameters: Normal allows continuous control as a pitch ratio. Micro allows for fine adjustments around Unison. Chromatic allows you to select intervals equal to the 12 note per octave scale.

parameters.name: Pitch A Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of feedback for Delay A.

parameters.name: Pitch B Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of feedback for Delay B.

performanceParams.name: Repeat
performanceParams.description: Press-and-hold for infinite repeat.

name: HarModulator
overview: Combines twin chromatic pitch shifters with modulation delivering effects from subtle to extreme.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Pitch A/B Mix
parameters.description: Ratio of Pitch A to B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the ratio of the level of Pitch A to Pitch B.

parameters.name: Pitch Shift A
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the pitch shift interval in semitone increments from down three octaves to up three octaves.

parameters.name: Pitch Shift B
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the pitch shift interval in semitone increments from down three octaves to up three octaves.

parameters.name: Delay A
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of time delay of the A pitch shifted output. With Tempo OFF, delay is displayed in ms. With Tempo Sync ON, delay can be sync’d to the tempo and is displayed as a rhythmic sub-division of the tempo beat value.

parameters.name: Delay B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of time delay of the B pitch shifted output. With Tempo Sync OFF, delay is displayed in ms. With Tempo Sync ON, delay can be sync’d to the tempo and is displayed as a rhythmic sub-division of the tempo beat value.

parameters.name: Modulation Depth
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount (or depth) of pitch modulation displayed in cents over a four octave range (two octaves down, two octaves up). Fine control for micro-pitch modulation is available and displayed in cents, ranging from -30 to +30 cents. When the modulation is a positive value the two voices will modulate in sync with each other; when the value is negative they will modulate out of sync.

parameters.name: Modulation Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the modulation rate. Note: If Enveloep is selected as the Mod Shape, then modulation is driven by the amplitude of the audio input and Modulation Rate becomes a Sensitivity control.

parameters.name: Modulation Shape
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the modulation shape. Select Envelop and your playing will drive the pitch modulation.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of feedback for Delays A and B.

performanceParams.name: Flex
performanceParams.description: Shifts both voices up one octave.

name: HarPeggiator
overview: Creates dual 16-step arpeggios combining pitch sequencer, rhythm sequencer, and effect sequencer.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Arpeggiator A/B Mix
parameters.description: Ratio between arpeggios A and B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the ratio of arpeggiator A to arpeggiator B.

parameters.name: Pitch Sequence A
parameters.fullDescription: See description and table above

parameters.name: Pitch Sequence B
parameters.fullDescription: See description and table above

parameters.name: Rhythm A
parameters.fullDescription: These controls select the rhythm/groove sequence for A/B. The rhythm sequences are a set of 21 selectable presets. The pitch sequences are numbered from 01 to 20, plus and additional random rhythm. Set the control to Off to turn off the rhythm sequence. With the rhythm sequence turned Off, all sixteen steps of the sequence are played at full amplitude.

parameters.name: Rhythm B
parameters.fullDescription: See the description for Rhythm A.

parameters.name: Dynamics
parameters.fullDescription: Sets attack and release time for the dynamics of the Rhythm and Effects. When set to minimum (-10), the audio takes the entire step length to fade in; at mid-range (0), the audio is present for the entire step duration; and at maximum (10), the audio is present for only 1/10th of the step’s duration. Note: This control has no effect when both Rhythm and Effect knobs are set to Off.

parameters.name: Step Length
parameters.description: Duration per step, ms or tempo-synced
parameters.fullDescription: With Tempo Sync OFF, sets the length of each of the 16 steps in ms. With Tempo Sync ON, sets the length of each step relative to the tap tempo (length of note e.g. whole, quarter, etc.).

parameters.name: Effect A
parameters.fullDescription: HarPeggiator lets you apply a sequence of filter, fuzz and/or glitch effects to each note of the 16-step sequence. The effect sequences are a set of 25 selectable presets. The effects are indicated by effect type: Filter 1-5 Fuzz 1-5 Glitch 1-5 All There are five filter effects, five fuzz effects and five glitch effects to choose from. Or, you can select one of four different types of random effect sequences: Random Filters Random Fuzz Random Glitches

parameters.name: Effect B
parameters.fullDescription: See the description for Effect A.

performanceParams.name: Restart
performanceParams.description: Restarts the sequence from the beginning.

name: MicroPitch
overview: Fine-resolution pitch shifter ideal for vocal doubling, tone fattening, and unique delays.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Relative level of the wet and dry signals.

parameters.name: Pitch Mix
parameters.description: Ratio of Pitch A to B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the ratio of the level of Pitch A to Pitch B.

parameters.name: Pitch A
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of pitch shift up for voice A, from Unison to +50 cents.

parameters.name: Pitch B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of pitch shift down for voice B, from Unison to -50 cents.

parameters.name: Delay A
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of time delay of the A pitch-shifted output. With Tempo Sync OFF, delay is displayed in ms. With Tempo Sync ON, delay can be sync’d to the tempo and is displayed as a rhythmic sub-division of the tempo beat value.

parameters.name: Delay B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of time delay of the B pitch-shifted output. With Tempo Sync OFF, delay is displayed in ms. With Tempo Sync ON, delay can be sync’d to the tempo and is displayed as a rhythmic sub-division of the tempo beat value.

parameters.name: Modulation Depth
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount (or depth) of pitch modulation around the current pitch for each voice. A value of 100 represents a bipolar full swing of the modulation from 0 cents to 2x Pitch. Lesser values scale proportionally.

parameters.name: Mod Source
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the modulation source. -Env: Increased volume decreases the Pitch modulation. LFO: Pitch modulation is controlled by the LFO rate set by the Mod Rate parameter. +Env: Increased volume increases the Pitch modulation.

parameters.name: Mod Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the LFO rate when LFO is selected as the Mod Source. 0.1 to 10 Hz

parameters.name: Mod Sens
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the sensitivity of the envelope follower when -Env or +Env is selected as the Mod Source.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of feedback for Delays A and B.

parameters.name: Tone
parameters.fullDescription: Applies filtering to voice A and voice B.

performanceParams.name: Flex
performanceParams.description: Doubles the pitch shift amount of both voices.

name: Octaver
overview: Synthesizes paired sub-harmonics one and two octaves below with octave fuzz generator and modulated filters.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Sub-Harmonic Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Controls mix of 1st and 2nd sub-harmonics (A and B). Note that Inputs 1 and Inputs 2 are not mixed.

parameters.name: Filter Center Frequency A
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the center frequency of the resonant filter for A.

parameters.name: Filter Center Frequency B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the center frequency of the resonant filter for B.

parameters.name: Filter Resonance A
parameters.fullDescription: Controls filter resonance for A. Note: After adjusting the filter’s center frequency and resonance, you may want to try modulating the filter.

parameters.name: Filter Resonance B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls filter resonance for B.

parameters.name: Envelope Filter Shift
parameters.fullDescription: Octaver allows your playing to vary the center frequency of the filters. This control adjusts the degree to which the input signal’s envelop shifts the filter’s center frequency.

parameters.name: Envelope Sensitivity
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the sensitivity of the frequency sweeps to the input signal level.

parameters.name: Distortion
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of distortion (fuzz).

parameters.name: Octave-Fuzz Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the mix of octaves and fuzz.

name: PitchFlex
overview: Designed for live use with expression pedal or footswitch, set pitch shift at each end of pedal travel.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Pitch Mix
parameters.description: Ratio of Pitch A to B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the ratio of the level of Pitch A to Pitch B.

parameters.name: Manual Shift
parameters.fullDescription: Sweeps the pitch from the ‘heel’ to ‘toe’ value. Map an expression pedal to this parameter and adjust it to perform the sweep.

parameters.name: Heel A
parameters.fullDescription: Sets pitch shift of the voice in the heel position. When ‘OFF’ is selected, the voice is muted at the heel position and the pitch is set to unison.

parameters.name: Heel B
parameters.fullDescription: Sets pitch shift of the voice in the heel position. When ‘OFF’ is selected, the voice is muted at the heel position and the pitch is set to unison.

parameters.name: Toe A
parameters.fullDescription: Sets pitch shift of the voice in the toe position. When ‘OFF’ is selected, the A pitch shifter is disabled at the toe position and toe is treated as unison.

parameters.name: Toe B
parameters.fullDescription: Sets pitch shift of the voice in the toe position. When ‘OFF’ is selected, the A pitch shifter is disabled at the toe position and toe is treated as unison.

parameters.name: Heel-to-toe glissando
parameters.fullDescription: This parameter is for use when using the Flex performance parameter, or an Auxiliary Switch to control the pitch change effect for voices A and B. Sets the time to move from ‘heel’ to ‘toe’. With Tempo Sync ON the maximum is ½ note.

parameters.name: Toe-to-heel glissando
parameters.fullDescription: Same as above, but set the time to move from ‘toe’ to ‘heel’.

parameters.name: Glissando Shape
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the ‘shape’ that the pitch modulation follows when using the Flex Switch. If set to Negative values, the pitch goes slowly towards ‘toe’ and quickly transitions to ‘heel’, Positive is the other way around, and 0 means the pitch shifts up and down linearly.

parameters.name: Low Pass Filter
parameters.fullDescription: A low pass filter to ‘darken’ the effect.

performanceParams.name: Flex
performanceParams.description: Sweep the pitch shift from ‘toe’ to ‘heel’ position.

name: PolyFlex
overview: Fully polyphonic version of PitchFlex allowing chord bending, dive bombs, and pedal-steel tones.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Detune
parameters.fullDescription: A detune amount applied to both the base shift amount and the Flex amount, from -50 cents to 50 cents.

parameters.name: Flex Time
parameters.description: Duration from shift to flex interval
parameters.fullDescription: How long it takes to get from the Shift interval to the Flex interval from 0 to 2 seconds.

parameters.name: Return Time
parameters.description: Duration from flex back to shift
parameters.fullDescription: How long it takes to return to the Shift interval from the Flex interval from 0 to 2 seconds.

parameters.name: Auto Engage
parameters.fullDescription: When on, the algorithm is bypassed until the Flex is engaged, or Manual Flex is above 0. This will be triggered by any of the available ways to Flex.

parameters.name: Auto Engage Hold Time
parameters.fullDescription: If Auto Engage is on, this parameter allows the pitch shifted effect to remain on for a period of time from 0 to 1 second. This can help the auto engage parameter from disengaging abruptly. The following parameters are available for both pitch A and B:

parameters.name: Level A
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the level of the pitch shifted voice.

parameters.name: Level B
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the level of the pitch shifted voice.

parameters.name: Shift A
parameters.fullDescription: The base pitch shift amount specified in musical intervals for each voice of shifting. -2 Oct, -P12, -P11, -Oct, -M7, -m7, -M6, -m6, -P5, -Tri, -P4, -M3, -m3, -M2, -m2, Uni, m2, M2, m3, M3, P4, Tri, P5, m6, M6, m7, M7, Oct, P11, P12, 2 Oct

parameters.name: Shift B
parameters.fullDescription: The base pitch shift amount specified in musical intervals for each voice of shifting. -2 Oct, -P12, -P11, -Oct, -M7, -m7, -M6, -m6, -P5, -Tri, -P4, -M3, -m3, -M2, -m2, Uni, m2, M2, m3, M3, P4, Tri, P5, m6, M6, m7, M7, Oct, P11, P12, 2 Oct

parameters.name: Flex A
parameters.fullDescription: The pitch shift amount to dive/bend to. -2 Oct, -P12, -P11, -Oct, -M7, -m7, -M6, -m6, -P5, -Tri, -P4, -M3, -m3, -M2, -m2, Uni, m2, M2, m3, M3, P4, Tri, P5, m6, M6, m7, M7, Oct, P11, P12, 2 Oct

parameters.name: Flex B
parameters.fullDescription: The pitch shift amount to dive/bend to. -2 Oct, -P12, -P11, -Oct, -M7, -m7, -M6, -m6, -P5, -Tri, -P4, -M3, -m3, -M2, -m2, Uni, m2, M2, m3, M3, P4, Tri, P5, m6, M6, m7, M7, Oct, P11, P12, 2 Oct

parameters.name: Pan A
parameters.fullDescription: Moves the pitch shifted voice left or right in the stereo field.

parameters.name: Pan B
parameters.fullDescription: Moves the pitch shifted voice left or right in the stereo field.

performanceParams.name: Flex A
performanceParams.description: Activates pitch bend for voice A, gliding from Shift A to Flex A interval.

performanceParams.name: Flex B
performanceParams.description: Activates pitch bend for voice B, gliding from Shift B to Flex B interval.

performanceParams.name: Flex Dual
performanceParams.description: Bends both voices simultaneously.

performanceParams.name: Freeze
performanceParams.description: Freezes the pitch-shifiting sound for pad like textures. The dry signal is still passed through. Note that if Auto Engage is On, you won’t hear the frozen signal when you are not flexing.

name: Polyphony
overview: Low-latency, high quality polyphonic pitch shifter leveraging SIFT technology with zero tracking errors.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Inst Type
parameters.fullDescription: Tunes the underlying pitch shifting Algorithm for either pitched or percussive instruments. Tip The Percussive instrument mode is designed to prioritize preserving the transients and tone of the original signal while also having less latency. For applications where you would like to re-tune a guitar or bass (Mix is 100% wet) Percussive mode may work better for small shift intervals. For larger intervals the quality of the shifted sound will be be better in the Pitched mode.

parameters.name: Feedback Sw
parameters.description: Pitch shifter inside or outside feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Places the pitch shifter inside or outside of the feedback path. Because the pitch shifting is a mono in, multi out effect, the feedback paths of the shifted voices are summed together first before being fed back to the pitch shifter. This causes the feedback to slowly fade towards the center even if the voices are panned hard left/right when the pitch shifter is inside the feedback path. There is also a gentle lowpass filter in this mode to help remove some of the very very high annoying stuff that can happen when shifting up over and over again. Note: Feedback amounts may have different effects in one mode vs the other.

parameters.name: Auto EQ
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the amount of automatic EQ that is applied to the shifted voices to help them sound more natural. When shifting down the auto eq brightens things up to try and preserve the transients of the original signal. When shifting up, the auto eq smooths out harsh squeaky sounds. The EQ is automatically adjusted based on the amount of shift applied in either direction. A default setting of 10 is recommended. The following parameters are available for both pitch A and B:

parameters.name: Level A
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the volume of the pitch.

parameters.name: Level B
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the volume of the pitch.

parameters.name: Detune A
parameters.fullDescription: The amount of detuning, from -50 cents to 50 cents.

parameters.name: Detune B
parameters.fullDescription: The amount of detuning, from -50 cents to 50 cents.

parameters.name: Delay A
parameters.fullDescription: The amount of delay, from 0 ms to 2 s.

parameters.name: Delay B
parameters.fullDescription: The amount of delay, from 0 ms to 2 s.

parameters.name: Feedback A
parameters.fullDescription: The amount of feedback for each voice. Behaves differently depending on how Feedback Sw is set.

parameters.name: Feedback B
parameters.fullDescription: The amount of feedback for each voice. Behaves differently depending on how Feedback Sw is set.

parameters.name: Pan A
parameters.fullDescription: Moves the pitch left or right in the stereo field.

parameters.name: Pan B
parameters.fullDescription: Moves the pitch left or right in the stereo field.

performanceParams.name: Freeze
performanceParams.description: Freezes the pitch-shifiting sound for pad like textures. The dry signal is still passed through.

name: Prism Shift
overview: Polyphonic effects generating 3 arpeggiated voices from a single chord with sophisticated tracking.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Arp Type
parameters.fullDescription: Sets which type of Arpeggio to use. The choices are: Rising Falling Falling/Rising Rising/Falling

parameters.name: Step Length
parameters.fullDescription: Time between successive notes in the arpeggio

parameters.name: Shift
parameters.fullDescription: Shift ratio for the low/high voices. E.g., if -1oct +1oct is used the high voice will be shifted up and octave and the low voice will be shifted down an octave. The choices are: -1oct +1oct -P4 +P5 -P5 +P4 +1oct +2oct +P5 +P12 +P4 +P11

parameters.name: Arp Order
parameters.fullDescription: Order of the voices in the arpeggio. E.g., if the Arp Type is rising and the Arp Order is L-M-H, then the arpeggio will rise from low-to-high frequencies. However, if the Arp Order is H-M-L, then the high voice will rise, following by a rising mid voice, and then a rising low voice. This creates a simultaneous ascending while descending sort of effect. The choices are: L-M-H L-H-M M-L-H M-H-L H-L-M H-M-L

parameters.name: Auto EQ
parameters.fullDescription: An Eq for the pitch shifted voices. A default setting of 10 is recommended. The following controls are available for all three voices:

parameters.name: Gain Low
parameters.fullDescription: A gain control for the specific voice.

parameters.name: Gain Mid
parameters.fullDescription: A gain control for the specific voice.

parameters.name: Gain High
parameters.fullDescription: A gain control for the specific voice.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: A feedback control for the specific voice.

parameters.name: Spread
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the stereo spread of the voices. When set to -1 the Low voice is panned hard left, the mid voice is panned center, and the high voice is panned hard right. When set to +1 this is reversed. Intermediate values morph between these two extremes.

parameters.name: Slew Time
parameters.fullDescription: A slew on the Shift control. A large value will cause changes to the Shift parameter to glissando.

performanceParams.name: Freeze
performanceParams.description: Freezes the “High” and “Low” pitch shifted voices, the “Mid” voice (which is not pitch shifted) is not frozen.

performanceParams.name: Shift M
performanceParams.description: Moves the current value of the shift knob 1 position clockwise, wrapping if necessary.

name: Quadravox
overview: Up to four pitch shifted voices with independent interval selection based on key and scale.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Pitch A+C/B+D Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the ratio of level Pitch A+C to Pitch B+D. With the knob set full counter-clockwise, PitchA + PitchC are set to equal level. Full clock-wise, sets Pitch B + Pitch D to equal levels. The ratio of level of Pitch A to Pitch C and of Pitch B to Pitch D are fixed at equal levels and cannot be changed.

parameters.name: Pitch Shift A
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the harmonic interval (pitch shift) for Pitch A. Set to minimum to turn OFF voice A.

parameters.name: Pitch Shift B
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the harmonic interval (pitch shift) for Pitch B. Set to minimum to turn OFF voice B.

parameters.name: Pitch Shift C
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the harmonic interval (pitch shift) for Pitch C. Set to minimum to turn OFF voice C.

parameters.name: Pitch Shift D
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the harmonic interval (pitch shift) for Pitch D. Set to minimum to turn OFF voice D.

parameters.name: Delay D
parameters.description: Maximum staggered delay, ms or tempo-synced
parameters.fullDescription: Quadravox’s delay controls work differently from those in the other effects. Quadravox’s four delays are not independently variable. Instead, they are staggered with A having the shortest delay, B longer than A, C longer than B and D the longest. The Delay D control is used to set the last delay. With Tempo OFF, delay is displayed in ms. With Tempo Sync ON, delay can be sync’d to the tempo and is displayed as a rhythmic sub-division of the tempo beat value.

parameters.name: Delay Grouping
parameters.fullDescription: Select the grouping of the four delays (A, B, C, D). The delays can be evenly spaced or spread out.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the key.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the scale. The supported scales are: Major Minor Dorian Phrygian Lydian Mixolydian Locrian Harmonic Minor Melodic Minor Whole Tone Enigmatic Neapolitan Hungarian

parameters.name: Quantization
parameters.fullDescription: Quantizes the notes that are not in the selected key scale to fit within the selected key and scale.

performanceParams.name: Learn Mode
performanceParams.description: Press and hold the Learn switch while playing a note and the H90 will set the key to that note.

name: Resonator
overview: Staggers 4 resonant comb filters for ambient, arpeggiated, or reverberant sounds.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.fullDescription: Total length of the delay line. This length is split into 8 subdivisions on which the comb filters can be staggered.

parameters.name: Rhythm
parameters.fullDescription: Represents the rhythm pattern of the comb filters. Each digit indicates the subdivision on which a comb filter is positioned. “1.3.5.7” will sound like even quarter notes since the four comb filters are evenly spaced on the 1st, 3rd, 5th, and 7th subdivisions.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: The feedback level of each of the comb filters. Feedback type 1 [FB1] maintains the pattern set by the rhythm knob, whereas feedback type 2 [FB2] degrades the pattern as it repeats.

parameters.name: Resonance
parameters.fullDescription: Affects how intensely the comb filters resonate. The comb filters will ring out more intensely as the resonance increases in either the positive or negative direction. Resonance set to 0 will acts as multi-tap delay without any additional resonant tones.

parameters.name: Reverb
parameters.description: Reverb amount in comb filter path
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of reverb in the comb filter path.

parameters.name: Note 1
parameters.fullDescription: Tunes the note values that trigger each respective comb filter. When resonance is positive, all integer multiples of this frequency will resonate. When the resonance is negative, only odd multiples of this frequency will resonate. These note values also affect the high and low pass filters surrounding each comb filter. When resonance is set to 0, these knobs can still be used to filter the delays.

parameters.name: Note 2
parameters.fullDescription: Same as Note 1.

parameters.name: Note 3
parameters.fullDescription: Same as Note 1.

parameters.name: Note 4
parameters.fullDescription: Same as Note 1.

name: Quadravox+
overview: 4-voice vocal harmonizer with creative delay and arpeggio effects, unison voice replacing dry signal for quantization and formant manipulation.

parameters.name: Unison Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Mixes in the a unison copy of the dry signal, 100% is entirely the unison signal. Note that there is no true dry signal, but your unison voice will sound more natural with quantization off and higher tuning speeds.

parameters.name: Unison Formant
parameters.fullDescription: Shift the formants of the unison voice from -600 to 600 c without adjusting the pitch.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the key.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the scale.

parameters.name: Quantization
parameters.fullDescription: Quantizes the notes that are not in the selected key scale to fit within the selected key and scale.

parameters.name: Formant Link
parameters.fullDescription: Automatically stretches or compresses the vocal formants in proportion to the pitch shift interval. Positive values will make the voice sound more throaty when pitching down and more chipmunky when shifting up. Negative values invert this relationship. Larger positive or negative values produce a more pronounced effect. A value of 0 preserves the natural formants of the voice (they are unaltered)

parameters.name: Pitch Mix
parameters.description: Controls level ratio A+C vs B+D
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the ratio of level Pitch A+C to Pitch B+D. With the knob set full counter-clockwise, PitchA + PitchC are set to equal level. Full clock-wise, sets Pitch B + Pitch D to equal levels. The ratio of level of Pitch A to Pitch C and of Pitch B to Pitch D are fixed at equal levels and cannot be changed.

parameters.name: Shift A
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the harmonic interval (pitch shift) for the Pitch. Set to minimum to turn any voice OFF.

parameters.name: Shift B
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the harmonic interval (pitch shift) for the Pitch. Set to minimum to turn any voice OFF.

parameters.name: Shift C
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the harmonic interval (pitch shift) for the Pitch. Set to minimum to turn any voice OFF.

parameters.name: Shift D
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the harmonic interval (pitch shift) for the Pitch. Set to minimum to turn any voice OFF.

parameters.name: Delay
parameters.description: Staggered across 4 voices, ms or tempo-synced
parameters.fullDescription: The four delays are not independently variable. Instead, they are staggered with A having the shortest delay, B longer than A, C longer than B and D the longest. The Delay control is used to set the last delay. With Tempo OFF, delay is displayed in ms. With Tempo Sync ON, delay can be sync’d to the tempo and is displayed as a rhythmic sub-division of the tempo beat value.

parameters.name: Division
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts how the Delay parameter is divided between the four delays (A, B, C, D). The delays can be evenly spaced or spread out.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Amount of Feedback for the delay line.

parameters.name: Spread
parameters.fullDescription: Pans the voices left/right.

parameters.name: Tuning Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Adjust the speed of pitch shifting from snappy, hard tuned quantization to more subtle pitch correction. 0 to 200ms

parameters.name: Detune
parameters.fullDescription: Equally detunes all voices +/- 50 c.

parameters.name: Auto EQ
parameters.fullDescription: Boosts both low and high frequencies which often sound quieter after pitch shifting. This can help the pitch shifted voices sit better in the mix.

performanceParams.name: Root Learn
performanceParams.description: Analyzes the current vocal pitch via footswitch and automatically updates the Root/Key parameter.

performanceParams.name: Glide
performanceParams.description: Shifts pitch to the designated glide interval, smoothly transitioning from current to target pitch.

performanceParams.name: Glide Mom
performanceParams.description: Momentary pitch shift to glide interval; returns when released.

name: VocalShift
overview: High quality vocal harmonizer with 3 voices, independent pitch and formant shifting, key/scale quantization.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/Dry mixer.

parameters.name: Root
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the root for the Scale parameter.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.fullDescription: Set the Scale that determines the pitch shifted intervals.

parameters.name: Shift A
parameters.fullDescription: Pitch shift interval.

parameters.name: Shift B
parameters.fullDescription: Pitch shift interval.

parameters.name: Shift C
parameters.fullDescription: Pitch shift interval.

parameters.name: Formant A
parameters.fullDescription: Formant shift amount. Positive values will make the voice sound more chipmunky, negative values will make the voice sound more throaty. Leaving this control at 0 will preserve the formants and give a more natural sound when changing the Shift parameter.

parameters.name: Formant B
parameters.fullDescription: Formant shift amount. Positive values will make the voice sound more chipmunky, negative values will make the voice sound more throaty. Leaving this control at 0 will preserve the formants and give a more natural sound when changing the Shift parameter.

parameters.name: Formant C
parameters.fullDescription: Formant shift amount. Positive values will make the voice sound more chipmunky, negative values will make the voice sound more throaty. Leaving this control at 0 will preserve the formants and give a more natural sound when changing the Shift parameter.

parameters.name: Formant Link
parameters.fullDescription: Automatically stretches or compresses the vocal formants in proportion to the pitch shift interval. Positive values will make the voice sound more throaty when pitching down and more chipmunky when shifting up. Negative values invert this relationship. Larger positive or negative values produce a more pronounced effect. A value of 0 preserves the natural formants of the voice (they are unaltered).

parameters.name: Gain A
parameters.fullDescription: Voice level.

parameters.name: Gain B
parameters.fullDescription: Voice level.

parameters.name: Gain C
parameters.fullDescription: Voice level.

parameters.name: Delay A
parameters.fullDescription: Delay time in ms or Tempo Synced.

parameters.name: Delay B
parameters.fullDescription: Delay time in ms or Tempo Synced.

parameters.name: Delay C
parameters.fullDescription: Delay time in ms or Tempo Synced.

parameters.name: Feedback A
parameters.fullDescription: Feedback amount.

parameters.name: Pan A
parameters.fullDescription: Stereo position (-50: left, 0: center, 50: right).

parameters.name: Pan B
parameters.fullDescription: Stereo position (-50: left, 0: center, 50: right).

parameters.name: Pan C
parameters.fullDescription: Stereo position (-50: left, 0: center, 50: right).

parameters.name: Detune
parameters.fullDescription: Applies equal detuning up and down to Voice B & C from 0 to 50c. 0 to -50c applies opposite detuning.

parameters.name: Tuning Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Adjust the speed of pitch shifting from snappy, hard tuned quantization to more subtle pitch correction. Note that tuning speed is applied whether quantization is on/off, and is an important parameter to tuning how the algorithms processed the vocal signal. 0 to 500ms.

performanceParams.name: Root Learn
performanceParams.description: Analyzes the currently sung note via footswitch and updates the Root parameter automatically.

performanceParams.name: Glide
performanceParams.description: Shifts pitch to the designated glide interval.

performanceParams.name: Glide M
performanceParams.description: Momentary pitch shift to glide interval; returns when released.

performanceParams.name: Hold M
performanceParams.description: Momentarily maintains the current voice pitches while preserving articulation nuances of the dry signal.

performanceParams.name: Hold
performanceParams.description: Latching version of Hold M; sustains voice pitches until disengaged.

name: VocalShiftMIDI
overview: Four voice version of VocalShift with MIDI control of pitch shift values for harmonies, chord stacks, and pitch correction.

parameters.name: Unison Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Mixes in the a unison copy of the dry signal, 100% is entirely the unison signal. Note that there is no true dry signal, but your unison voice will sound more natural with quantization off and higher tuning speeds.

parameters.name: Unison Formant
parameters.fullDescription: Shift the formants of the unison voice from -600 to 600 c without adjusting the pitch.

parameters.name: Tuning Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Adjust the speed of pitch shifting from snappy, hard tuned quantization to more subtle pitch correction. 0 to 500ms.

parameters.name: Gate
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the threshold of the noise gate.

parameters.name: Gate Attack
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the attack time for the noise gate.

parameters.name: Gate Release
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the release time for the noise gate.

parameters.name: Vibe Rate
parameters.fullDescription: The rate of the vibrato applied to the MIDI pitch shifted voices. 0 to 8 Hz.

parameters.name: Vibe Depth
parameters.fullDescription: The depth of the vibrato applied to the MIDI pitch shifted voices. 0 to 50 c.

parameters.name: Vibe Slew
parameters.fullDescription: The time it takes before vibrato is applied. 0 to 2000 ms.

parameters.name: Glide
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the slew time between note changes for each voice. 0 to 5000 octaves per millisecond.

parameters.name: Delay
parameters.fullDescription: Global Delay time for all voices in ms or Tempo Synced.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Feedback amount for the delay line.

parameters.name: Formant Link
