parameters.fullDescription: Automatically stretches or compresses the vocal formants in proportion to the pitch shift interval. Positive values will make the voice sound more throaty when pitching down and more chipmunky when shifting up. Negative values invert this relationship. Larger positive or negative values produce a more pronounced effect. A value of 0 preserves the natural formants of the voice (they are unaltered).

parameters.name: Spread
parameters.fullDescription: Narrow / Medium / Wide. Pans every other voice left or right.

parameters.name: Attack
parameters.fullDescription: Envelope attack time of the pitch shifted voice. 0 to 3000 ms.

parameters.name: Release
parameters.fullDescription: Envelope release time of the pitch shifted voice. 0 to 3000 ms.

parameters.name: Hold
parameters.fullDescription: Off will only output pitch shifted audio while an incoming MIDI note is ON. On will ignore MIDI note OFF and hold the value of the incoming MIDI note until it receives another MIDI note. Attack/Release times do not apply when Sustain is latching.

parameters.name: Velocity Sens
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the sensitivity of incoming MIDI velocity data. At 100, MIDI velocity messages are passed thru unchanged. At 0, MIDI velocity will be ignored and the pitch shifted voices will always play at full volume. At 50, the minimum velocity value would be 64. Use this control if you are unable to adjust the velocity curve on your MIDI controller.

parameters.name: Auto EQ
parameters.fullDescription: Boosts both low and high frequencies which often sound quieter after pitch shifting. This can help the pitch shifted voices sit better in the mix.

parameters.name: Latency Mode
parameters.fullDescription: Low/High - Higher latency may result in improved performance with a tradeoff of more noticeable latency. The high latency setting is recommended when using extreme formant settings. Low introduces ~25 ms of latency, while High introduces ~50 ms of latency.

performanceParams.name: Root Learn
performanceParams.description: Analyzes the currently sung note and updates the Root parameter automatically.

performanceParams.name: Freeze
performanceParams.description: Preserves the input signal for pad creation and MIDI-controlled pitch shifting over the frozen audio.

performanceParams.name: Freeze M
performanceParams.description: Momentary freeze of input signal; releases when footswitch is released.

name: VocalTune
overview: Stripped down version of VocalShift for vocal correction and hard tune effects with integrated EQ and compressor.

parameters.name: Root
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the root for the Scale parameter.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.fullDescription: Set the Scale that determines the intervals that the vocals will be quantized to.

parameters.name: Tuning Reference
parameters.fullDescription: Set the reference point for the key/scale quantization.

parameters.name: Quant Error
parameters.fullDescription: Allows small pitch variations to not be quantized. Useful for vocals with vibrato when quantization is on. 0 to 50 c.

parameters.name: Formant
parameters.fullDescription: Shift the formants from -600 to 600 c without adjusting the pitch.

parameters.name: Tuning Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Adjust the speed of quantization from snappy, robotic sounding quantization to more subtle pitch correction. 0 to 500 ms.

parameters.name: Bass
parameters.fullDescription: Boost/Cut the bass frequencies from -18 to +12 dB.

parameters.name: Treble
parameters.fullDescription: Boost/Cute the treble frequencies -18 to +12 dB.

parameters.name: Threshold
parameters.fullDescription: Set the threshold of the compressor.

parameters.name: Ratio
parameters.fullDescription: Ratio of the compressor, ranging from 1:1 to 20:1.

parameters.name: Attack
parameters.fullDescription: Compressor attack time, 0.1 to 100 ms.

parameters.name: Release
parameters.fullDescription: Compressor release time, 1-1000 ms.

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.fullDescription: Compressor makeup gain, -16 to +12 dB.

parameters.name: Gate
parameters.fullDescription: Threshold of noise gate.

name: Looper
overview: 60 seconds of stereo recording at full quality and up to 480 seconds at reduced quality with variable-speed playback, overdubbing, and MIDI sync.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Mix control between the Dry audio input and Looper playback.

parameters.name: Loop Max-Length
parameters.description: Maximum loop length based on recording speed
parameters.fullDescription: When the Loop is Empty, sets the Maximum allowed Loop Length. Note that audio recording quality is degraded at slower recording speeds (1/2x and 1/4x). The maximum loop length is determined by the setting of the Rec Speed parameter as follows: 60 sec at 2x 120 sec at 1x 240 sec at 1/2x 480 sec at 1/4x When the Loop contains audio, the Max-Length parameter cannot be adjusted. Note With Tempo Mode On, the range of the Max-Length parameter depends on the H90’s Tempo BPM value. Lower BPM values will not fill up the entire parameter range.

parameters.name: Loop Play-Start Point
parameters.fullDescription: When using Tempo Sync, the minimum length of audio that can be played out is 1 beat so, the Play-Start Point will display in beats allowing you start playback from 0 beats up to Loop Length minus 1 beat. For example, if you recorded an 8 beat loop, this will range from 0 to 7 beats. Changing the Play-Start Point during Playback will apply the next time the Loop comes around and will maintain perfect beat sync with the Tempo Source.

parameters.name: Loop Play-Length
parameters.fullDescription: When using Tempo Sync, the minimum length of audio that can be played out is 1 beat so, the Play-Length will display in beats allowing playback lengths of 1 beat up to the Loop Length. For example, if you recorded an 8 beat loop, the Play-Length will range from 1 to 8 beats. Changing the Play-Length during Playback will apply the next time the Loop comes around and will maintain perfect beat sync with your Tempo Source. Of course, new Play-Lengths that don’t evenly divide the total Loop Length will cause the loop to “walk” the downbeat, thus creating interesting poly-rhythms against an existing pattern.

parameters.name: Loop Decay Rate
parameters.fullDescription: When dubbing you may want the original saved audio to persist as you add new sounds. Of course, indefinitely adding new signals will eventually result in ‘mud’ (the “Crayola” effect). The Decay Rate control allows the saved audio to fade as you dub new material. The Decay Rate is adjustable from 0% [DCY: 0] to 100% [DCY:100]. When set to 0%, the loop never decays. When set to 100% the previously saved audio decays completely each time through the loop when dubbing. In other words, the looped audio is only played once. The Loop Decay Rate control has no effect on normal Playback, only dubbing.

parameters.name: Dubbing Mode
parameters.fullDescription: The Dubbing Mode choices are: Latch - Press the footswitch once to start dubbing and again to stop. Punch - Dub only while the footswitch is held down. Repl-Latch - Press the footswitch once to start replacing the loop audio and again to stop. Repl-Punch - Replace the loop audio only while the footswitch is held down.

parameters.name: Playmode
parameters.fullDescription: Playmode affects three actions of the Looper: the action when Recording reaches Max-Length, the action when Playing reaches the Play-Length, and the action of the PLAY switch.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.fullDescription: When set to Smooth, resolution is 1%. The other Depth control settings allow you to select the Play Speed in musical intervals as follows (a negative value corresponds to Reverse Play, and all resolutions have 0% in the middle for a full Pause):

parameters.name: Record Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the speed at which the initial loop is recorded. Slower speeds allow for longer loop lengths but at a lower fidelity. 2x – Double 1x – Normal 1/2x – Half 1/4x – Quarter

parameters.name: Play Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the playback speed of the loop. Play Speed resolution is dependent on the setting of the Resolution control. A negative speed with Empty causes playback to automatically start in the Reverse direction after the loop is closed, either through a PLAY button press or the Loop and Autoplay settings on the Playmode knob. After a loop is recorded, Play Speed controls the speed of Loop playback AND dubbing over the full range of speeds allowing for continuous real-time scrubbing from one octave up in Reverse Play (-200%), to one octave up in Forward Play (200%), with a pause (0%) directly in the middle (knob set to 12 o’clock).

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the tone of the looped audio. Tone control filters are placed at both the input and output of the Looper. This allows you to control the tone of the audio that you’re recording and then independently control the tone on playback. Turn to the left to cut low frequencies and to the right to cut high frequencies. For flat response, set the knob to 12 o’clock.

performanceParams.name: Record
performanceParams.description: Press the Footswitch to start recording a loop.

performanceParams.name: Play
performanceParams.description: Press the Footswitch to play a loop that has been recorded. The Playmode parameter will determine how this works.

performanceParams.name: Stop
performanceParams.description: Press the Footswitch to stop playback of the loop.

performanceParams.name: Empty
performanceParams.description: Press the Footswitch to empty the loop.

performanceParams.name: Undo/Redo
performanceParams.description: Press the Footswitch to undo the last overdub. Press it again to redo.

performanceParams.name: Direction
performanceParams.description: Toggles the direction of the loop playback between forwards/backwards.

performanceParams.name: Speed
performanceParams.description: Toggles the speed of the loop between full speed and half speed. At half speed the range of the Play Speed parameter will be limited to +/- 100.

name: Chorus
overview: Creates the sound of multiple instruments by randomly modulating delay lines and panning voices in stereo.

parameters.name: Intensity
parameters.fullDescription: Dry / wet mix.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the chorus type. The choices are: Liquid, Organic, Shimmer, Classic. Each type has a different chorus character and affects how the Feedback parameter behaves.

parameters.name: Depth
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the modulation sweep range from narrow to wide.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the modulation sweep rate. Speed becomes Sensitivity when Shape is set to Envelope or ADSR.

parameters.name: Shape
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the waveform, or source, of the modulation. The choices are: Sine Triangle Peak Random Square Ramp SampHold

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Controls feedback for Liquid and Shimmer. For Organic, used to scale a manual delay offset. For Classic, used to control a filter.

parameters.name: Depth Mod
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of modulation of the Depth parameter. Analogous to AM (Amplitude Modulation).

parameters.name: Speed Mod
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of modulation of the Speed parameter. Analogous to FM (Frequency Modulation).

parameters.name: Mod Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the secondary LFO rate – determines how fast the Depth Mod and Speed Mod “wiggle” their targets. Ranges from 1/8th to 8x the Speed value. Mod Rate becomes Mod Sens when Mod Source is set to Envelope or ADSR.

parameters.name: Mod Source
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the secondary LFO waveform, or source. The choices are: Sine Triangle Peak Random Square Ramp SampHold

performanceParams.name: Retrigger
performanceParams.description: Retriggers the primary and secondary modulation LFOs to the beginning of their cycles. Useful for re-syncing during playback, or creative effects.

performanceParams.name: Speed/Brake
performanceParams.description: The Brake engages while this switch is pressed. Short-press to toggle between Fast and Slow. Long-press to engage Brake.

performanceParams.name: Fast/Slow
performanceParams.description: Press to toggle between Fast and Slow, which slows the primary and secondary LFOs by a predetermined factor. The Brake does not engage while this switch is pressed.

performanceParams.name: Brake M
performanceParams.description: Slows the LFOs at a constant rate and pauses the LFOs until the switch is released.

name: Even-Vibe
overview: Stereo emulation of the classic Shin-ei Uni-Vibe with independent LFO control and envelope follower.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the mix between the unprocessed input and the modulated output. Set to 50% for a Uni-Vibe in chorus mode. Set to 100% for Uni-Vibe in vibrato mode.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the rate of the modulation. Tempo Sync off, Hertz. On, subdivisions. Like a classic Uni-Vibe, Speed will also subtly effect the intensity of the modulation. Don’t overthink it.

parameters.name: Intensity
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the depth of the modulation.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the stereo width of the output.

parameters.name: Env Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts how much the envelope controls the rate of the modulation. Negative values will result in slower modulation while the envelope is open, positive values will result in faster modulation.

parameters.name: Env Inten
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts how much the envelope controls the depth of the modulation. Negative values will result in less intensity of the modulation while the envelope is open, positive values will result in more intensity.

parameters.name: Softclip
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the amount of gentle transistor soft clipping.

performanceParams.name: Retrigger
performanceParams.description: Retriggers the LFO to the beginning of the cycle. Useful for re-syncing during playback, or creative effects.

name: Flanger
overview: Creates deeper, more numerous frequency notches than phasing through modulated delay line mixing.

parameters.name: Intensity
parameters.fullDescription: Effect level.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.fullDescription: Selects flanger topology: Positive (non-inverted), Negative (inverted for deeper nulls), Jet (extreme metallic effect), Thru-0 (dual delay lines flanging through zero for dramatic sweeps).

parameters.name: Depth
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the modulation sweep range from narrow to wide.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the modulation sweep rate. Speed becomes Sensitivity when Shape is set to Envelope or ADSR.

parameters.name: Shape
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the waveform, or source, of the modulation. The choices are: Sine Triangle Peak Random Square Ramp SampHold

parameters.name: Delay Offset
parameters.fullDescription: Set Delay offset.

parameters.name: Depth Mod
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of modulation of the Depth parameter. Analogous to AM (Amplitude Modulation).

parameters.name: Speed Mod
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of modulation of the Speed parameter. Analogous to FM (Frequency Modulation).

parameters.name: Mod Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the secondary LFO rate – determines how fast the Depth Mod and Speed Mod “wiggle” their targets. Ranges from 1/8th to 8x the Speed value. Mod Rate becomes Mod Sens when Mod Source is set to Envelope or ADSR.

parameters.name: Mod Source
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the secondary LFO waveform, or source. The choices are: Sine Triangle Peak Random Square Ramp SampHold

performanceParams.name: Retrigger
performanceParams.description: Retriggers the primary and secondary modulation LFOs to the beginning of their cycles. Useful for re-syncing during playback, or creative effects.

performanceParams.name: Speed/Brake
performanceParams.description: The Brake engages while this switch is pressed. Short-press to toggle between Fast and Slow. Long-press to engage Brake.

performanceParams.name: Fast/Slow
performanceParams.description: Press to toggle between Fast and Slow, which slows the primary and secondary LFOs by a predetermined factor. The Brake does not engage while this switch is pressed.

performanceParams.name: Brake M
performanceParams.description: Slows the LFOs at a constant rate and pauses the LFOs until the switch is released.

name: Harmadillo
overview: Flexible harmonic tremolo splitting signals into low/high frequency bands with inverted LFO and envelope control.

parameters.name: Depth
parameters.fullDescription: This is the depth of the low and high bands of the tremolo. At 0, the tremolo will have no effect on the volume of the bands. At 100, the bands will be completely faded in and out.

parameters.name: Rate
parameters.fullDescription: This is the base rate of the tremolo. When Tempo Sync is ON, this becomes a multiplier on the tapped BPM value. The Env Rate control can change the apparent rate, so if the rate that you are hearing is different from the value of the Rate control, try setting the value of Env Rate to 0.

parameters.name: Shape
parameters.description: Sine/Fat Sine/Phat Sine/Triangle/Ramp Dn/Ramp Up/Pulse variations/Lump/Rump/Slope
parameters.fullDescription: The shape of the tremolo waveform. The same shape is used for both high and low bands. The shapes describe what happens to the low band, since it’s usually the main part of your sound; the high band will change in the opposite direction. Options are: Sine, Fat Sine, Phat Sine, Triangle, Ramp Dn, Ramp Up, Pulse 25, Pulse 50, Pulse 75, Lump, Rump, Slope.

parameters.name: X-Over
parameters.fullDescription: This control determines where in the frequency spectrum the low band ends and the high frequency band begins. In practice, each band rolls off around the crossover frequency, so they overlap a bit. The X-Over control allows you to adjust the amount of overlap; see below. To mimic an ordinary tremolo, set X-Over to its maximum value of 12,000 Hz. Most of an electric guitar’s sound spectrum is below 6,000 Hz. Any audio above 12,000 Hz (i.e., hardly any sound) will be heard in the high band, and everything else will be heard in the “low” band. Change X-Over gradually to morph into and out of an ordinary tremolo sound. X-Over is especially interesting when Shape is set to an asymmetric shape such as Ramp Up. This means that for each cycle of the tremolo the portion of the signal below the X-Over value will be faded in and the portion of the signal above the X-Over value will be faded out. If you start playing a scale below the X-Over frequency and continue playing above it, the shape of the tremolo will appear to change as you cross over the X-Over frequency!

parameters.name: X-Overlap
parameters.fullDescription: This control adjusts the amount of overlap between the high and low bands. Negative values will produce a cut at the crossover frequency, and positive values will produce a boost at the crossover frequency. To explore the effect of this control set: X-Over to 100 Depth to 0 Env Depth to 0 Drive to 0 Env X-Over to 0 This removes the tremolo effect so you can hear the filtering. Slowly sweep the X-Over value from 100 Hz to 3,000 Hz as you play a repeated note, and listen for a boost at the crossover frequency. Harmonic tremolo effects often have a scoop in the midrange near their crossover. You can emulate this by using negative values for X-Over. To dial in classic sounds, set Env X-Over to 0, set the X-Over frequency between 400 Hz and 900 Hz, and adjust X-Over as needed.

parameters.name: Drive
parameters.fullDescription: This control adds warmth to the signal by mimicking the behavior of a tube amplifier’s harmonic tremolo.

parameters.name: Env Depth
parameters.fullDescription: This control uses the amplitude envelope of the input to increase or decrease the tremolo’s depth. Positive values increase the depth of the tremolo when you attack a note. The depth will return to the level set by the Depth control as the note decays. Additionally, louder notes will have greater depth than softer notes. Negative values reduce the tremolo when you attack a note, increasing the clarity of your attacks and making sustained notes more expressive over time. Playing louder will reduce the depth of the tremolo, and you can use large negative values to create tremolos that only appear when the input is soft. The Env Depth control covers a large range, so we recommend starting with values closer to 0, and then adjusting the control as needed.

parameters.name: Env Rate
parameters.fullDescription: This control uses the amplitude envelope of the input signal to affect the rate of the tremolo. With positive values, the tremolo jumps up in frequency when you attack a note and gradually returns to the original rate set by the Rate or Tap Tempo controls. The harder you play, the longer it will take to return. Negative values will temporarily reduce the rate of the tremolo when you attack a note, and the rate will return to the value set by the RATE knob or Tap Tempo as the note decays. With larger negative values and high Rate values, you can use this control to create bouncing-ball tremolo type effects. This is especially effective with the Shape control set to Pulse, Ramp Dn, Ramp Up, or Slope 0. The Env Rate control covers a large range. At 100%, it can push the internal LFO rate up to 80 Hz, so we recommend starting with values closer to 0, and then adjusting the control as needed.

parameters.name: Env X-Over
parameters.fullDescription: This control uses the amplitude envelope to affect the crossover frequency. To create a swept filter effect similar to an autowah, set X-Overlap to 100, set the X-Over frequency to 200 Hz, and then increase the amount of the Env X-Over control as necessary. The frequency of the crossover filter will now track the loudness of the input signal. You can create a single-notch phaser by turning Depth to 0, X-Overlap to -100, X-Over to 3500, and then set Env X-Over to a medium negative value, adjusting to taste. Once you have a sound you like, try slowly turning up the Depth control. Try playing long chords with these settings. In addition to the phasing effect, you will now notice that the tremolo also seems to change as a chord dies out. This is because the crossover frequency returns to the high X-Over value (3500 Hz) as the chord decays. Try setting Shape to an asymmetric waveform (e.g., Ramp Dn) to produce waveform morphing effects as the crossover frequency changes.

parameters.name: Tone
parameters.fullDescription: A tone control for shaping the high or low end of the output signal. Negative values roll off high frequencies (High Cut), and positive values roll off low frequencies (Low Cut).

performanceParams.name: Retrigger
performanceParams.description: Retriggers the LFO to the beginning of the cycle. Useful for re-syncing during playback, or creative effects.

name: Instant Flanger
overview: Authentic emulation of the original 1975 studio rackmount with vintage tape flanging character.

parameters.name: Depth
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the mix of wet and dry signals. At 0% the output is solely the wet signal. At 100% the output is the sum of the wet and dry signals. At −100% the output is the sum of the wet signal and the inverted dry signal. Note Many modulation-type effects provide mix controls. The Instant Flanger has a mix knob too, but rather than being labeled Mix, it is called Depth. Why is it called Depth, you ask? As you add more of the dry signal to the wet signal, nulls appear in the output spectrum. These nulls get deeper as the two signals approach equal amplitude. Hence you are controlling the depth of the nulls!

parameters.name: Source
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the source of the modulation. This can be any combination of the following: Oscillator, Manual, Envelope.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the stereo width of the flanging: Shallow, Deep, and Wide. Note The original Instant Flanger had two unique outputs called Main and Aux. The Main output used two bucket brigade devices in series, while the Aux output only used a single Bucket Brigade. This means the delay times of the Main output are roughly double that of the Aux output. The Mode control gives you access to which of these outputs is used by the Algorithm, and it can drastically change the sound. In Shallow mode, all outputs will correspond to the Aux output of the hardware unit. In Deep mode, all outputs will correspond to the Main output of the hardware unit. In Wide mode, the Main output is routed to the left channel and the Aux output is routed to the right channel. Because of the different delay lengths and the fact that Main and Aux are out of phase with each other, Wide mode will sound like it is panning the signal to the left or right, depending on how the Depth knob is set. Note that if you are using a mono output, Wide mode will be the same as Deep mode.

parameters.name: Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the rate of the LFO between 0.01 Hz and 20 Hz.

parameters.name: LFO Width
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the range of the sweep of the Flanger’s LFO. 100 is the widest possible sweep and equivalent to the range of the original hardware.

parameters.name: Env Thresh
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the threshold of the envelope follower, between −60 dB and 0 dB. An input signal will cause the biggest phase shift when it reaches the threshold level.

parameters.name: Env Release
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the release time of the envelope follower. Release times can vary from 10 milliseconds to 10 seconds.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the feedback of the flanger output. 0% sends none of the output back to the input to the bucket brigades, and 100% sends the maximum amount of output back to the input of the bucket brigades.

parameters.name: Low Cut
parameters.fullDescription: Applies a high-pass filter to the input signal before it is delayed. The original signal is still mixed with the output of the delays, but the flanging effect only acts on high frequencies.

parameters.name: Manual
parameters.fullDescription: Provides manual control of the flanging. 0% corresponds to setting the bucket brigades to their longest delay times, while 100% sets them to their shortest.

performanceParams.name: Retrigger
performanceParams.description: Retriggers the LFO to the beginning of the cycle. Useful for re-syncing during playback, or creative effects.

name: Instant Phaser
overview: Modeled on the original 1972 hardware unit with Age knob for tonal character ranging from factory-fresh to aged.

parameters.name: Depth
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the mix of wet and dry signals. At 0% the output is solely the phase shifted signal. At 100% the output is the addition of the phase shifted signal and the input. This will cause a gain of 6dB at certain frequencies depending on the phase shift.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the feedback of the phase shift sections. 0% sends none of the output back to the input to the phase shift sections and 100% sends the maximum amount of output back to the input of the phase shift sections.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the stereo width of the phasing: Shallow, Deep, and Wide Note The original Instant Phaser had two unique outputs called Main and Aux. The main output had two extra stages of phase shifting compared to the aux out, which means the two outputs are 180 degrees out of phase with each other. The Mode control gives you access to which of these outputs is used by the Algorithm, and it can drastically change the sound. In Shallow mode, all outputs will correspond to the Aux output of the hardware unit. In Deep mode, all outputs will correspond to the Main output of the hardware unit. In Wide mode, the Main output is routed to the left channel and the Aux output is routed to the right channel. Note that if you are using a mono output, Wide mode will be the same as Deep mode.

parameters.name: Age
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the age of the electrical components that make up the Instant Phaser. 0% is a factory fresh unit from 1971, 25% is the box we modeled as it is now, 100% is a very ungracefully aged Instant Phaser.

parameters.name: Source
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the source of the modulation. This can be any combination of the following: Oscillator, Manual, Envelope.

parameters.name: LFO Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the rate of the LFO from 0.01Hz to 20Hz

parameters.name: LFO Width
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the range of the sweep of the Phaser’s LFO. 100 is the widest possible sweep and equivalent to the range of the original hardware.

parameters.name: Env Thresh
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the threshold of the envelope follower. This is variable from -60dB to -0dB.

parameters.name: Env Release
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the release time of the envelope follower. Release times can vary from 10 milliseconds to 10 seconds.

parameters.name: Manual
parameters.fullDescription: Provides manual control of phasing. 0% moves the nulls in the frequency response to their highest position in frequency, 100% moves them to their lowest position in frequency.

performanceParams.name: Retrigger
performanceParams.description: Retriggers the LFO to the beginning of the cycle. Useful for re-syncing during playback, or creative effects.

name: ModFilter
overview: Modulated filters with selectable types and dual LFO modulation for swept, stereo-panned filter effects.

parameters.name: Intensity
parameters.fullDescription: Effect level. Controls a combination of base filter frequency and resonance.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the filter type. The choices are: Lowpass, Bandpass, Highpass.

parameters.name: Depth
parameters.description: Modulation sweep range and stereo offset
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the modulation sweep range from narrow to wide. Controls the frequency offset of the left and right channels to create a stereo image.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the modulation sweep rate. Speed becomes Sensitivity when Shape is set to Envelope or ADSR.

parameters.name: Shape
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the waveform, or source, of the modulation. The choices are: Sine Triangle Peak Random Square Ramp SampHold

parameters.name: Stereo Width
parameters.fullDescription: In mono, this control is not used. In stereo, this control shifts the phase of the right channel’s LFO creating a tremolo that will move from left to right in the stereo field. When set to Max, the right channel will be 180 degrees out of phase with the left, creating an autopanner.

parameters.name: Depth Mod
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of modulation of the Depth parameter. Analogous to AM (Amplitude Modulation).

parameters.name: Speed Mod
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of modulation of the Speed parameter. Analogous to FM (Frequency Modulation).

parameters.name: Mod Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the secondary LFO rate – determines how fast the Depth Mod and Speed Mod “wiggle” their targets. Ranges from 1/8th to 8x the Speed value. Mod Rate becomes Mod Sens when Mod Source is set to Envelope or ADSR.

parameters.name: Mod Source
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the secondary LFO waveform, or source. The choices are: Sine Triangle Peak Random Square Ramp SampHold

performanceParams.name: Retrigger
performanceParams.description: Retriggers the primary and secondary modulation LFOs to the beginning of their cycles. Useful for re-syncing during playback, or creative effects.

performanceParams.name: Speed/Brake
performanceParams.description: The Brake engages while this switch is pressed. Short-press to toggle between Fast and Slow. Long-press to engage Brake.

performanceParams.name: Fast/Slow

parameters.fullDescription: How much compression is applied. This is based off of the compression section of the DBX compander found in many professional tape machines. NOTE: Gain is automatically applied after the compression to try and maintain constant signal level. The Compression knob has been tuned for both Guitar and Bass. It is especially important to have the Instrument Type set correctly for Bass input and high Compression amounts.

parameters.name: Rec Drive
parameters.fullDescription: How much gain is applied before going in to the tape emulation.

parameters.name: Tape Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the nominal speed at which the two tape machines are running. This affects the tone of the signal.

parameters.name: W&F Amount
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the amount of wow and flutter applied. NOTE: With no offset, or no flanging engaged this will sound like detuning. With offset, or a flange engaged this sounds like modulation.

parameters.name: W&F Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the speed of the wow and flutter. 10 is a faster, more fluttery sound and 0 is more wowy.

parameters.name: Filter

parameters.name: Width
parameters.fullDescription: When stereo outputs are connected this pans the two tape machines to opposite sides. Note: When Offset is 0.0, or only a single output is connected, this control will do nothing.

parameters.name: Machine 2 Offset
parameters.fullDescription: Controls how delayed Machine 2 is from Machine 1 by adjusting its speed. At 0, the machines are running perfectly in sync. 10 adds enough delay to get into slapback territory. NOTE: When no offset is applied, a default range is used when using the Flange performance switches. If an offset is applied, then the offset is treated as the range.

parameters.name: Flange Length
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the rate of the flange performance parameters. This represents the time it takes for the flange to travel 1/2 of its period.

performanceParams.name: Flange M
performanceParams.description: A momentary performance switch to engage the flange. This changes Machine 2’s speed by an amount determined by either the offset, or a default setting, and a rate determined by the flange length knob. Hold it down and it’s like your thumb is on the reel, lift up and the speed of Machine 2 will return to its original setting.

performanceParams.name: Flange

name: Tricerachorus
overview: Bucket-brigade tri-chorus with three 120°-offset voices plus MicroPitch detuning.

parameters.name: Chorus Mix
parameters.description: Global wet/dry or Vibrato at 100 or Chorale mode
parameters.fullDescription: Global mix control for chorusing and has two modes, Chorus and Chorale. Chorus and Chorale Mix levels are independent of Detune Mix (see below). Chorus Mode (0 - 99 range on left half of the knob), all three chorus channel mixes, Left, Center, and Right are affected together. At 100 (Vibrato), no dry signal is present. In Chorus mode, LFO shapes are triangular from a range of 0 to 75. After 75, the LFO morphs from triangular to sinusoidal at 100. Chorale Mode (99 - 0 range on right half of the knob) adds two fixed rate LFOs, one slow and one fast, to the primary LFOs creating an effect similar to combining the Preset and Manual modes on the DYTRONICS TriChorus . This increases the complexity of the modulation, producing a richer sound. In Chorale mode, all LFO shapes are sinusoidal throughout the range of the control.

parameters.name: Rate
parameters.fullDescription: This is the base rate of the chorus modulation, from 0.1 Hz to 20 Hz. When Tempo Sync is On, this becomes a multiplier on the tapped BPM value. The Env Rate control can change the apparent rate, so if the rate that you are hearing is different from the value of the Rate control, try setting the value of Env Rate to 0.

parameters.name: Depth L
parameters.fullDescription: Chorus modulation depth of the Left voice. From Off, to 100. When Off, the voice is removed from the mix, and the levels of the other voices are automatically adjusted to preserve the wet-dry blend. In mono to stereo routing, if Depth R is Off but Depth L is active, the chorused signal will be routed to the left and the dry signal will be routed to the right. This is a classic technique for producing a wider stereo image.

parameters.name: Depth C
parameters.fullDescription: Chorus modulation depth of the Center voice. From Off, to 100. When Off, the voice is removed from the mix, and the levels of the other voices are automatically adjusted to preserve the wet-dry blend.

parameters.name: Depth R
parameters.fullDescription: Chorus modulation depth of the Right voice. From Off, to 100. When Off, the voice is removed from the mix, and the levels of the other voices are automatically adjusted to preserve the wet-dry blend. In mono to stereo routing, if Depth L is OFF but Depth R is active, the chorused signal will be routed to the right and the dry signal will be routed to the left. This is a classic technique for producing a wider stereo image.

parameters.name: Delay
parameters.fullDescription: When Chorus Mix is in Chorus Mode, DELAY sets the minimum delay time for all voices, ranging from 0.39 mS to 200 mS. Short delays can be used to create light flanging. Typical chorus delays range from 1.5-10 mS. You can create a chorused slapback sound by using values between 50 and 100 mS. When “Chorus Mix / Vibrato / Chorale Mix” is in Chorale Mode, the delay amount for each voice becomes a function of the Delay setting and the Depth level of each voice. In this case, the Delay becomes a range of possible delay amount for each voice. As you increase the Depth of a voice, its delay amount decreases.

parameters.name: Detune Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Mix control for the Detune section of the Algorithm. The Detuners are fed from the stereo output of the Chorus voices. Detune Mix controls the stereo left and right channels at the same time and is independent of “Chorus Mix / Vibrato / Chorale Mix”.

parameters.name: Detune
parameters.fullDescription: Controls both detune amounts for the left and right channels. Range is +/- 40 cents. Left and right channels get opposing amounts of detune (for example -30L/+30R). For enhanced versatility, the channels can be set with different opposing amounts. Left channel is the base detune amount and right channel can be adjusted around an 8 cent opposing window (for example -16L/+12R to -16L/+19R).

parameters.name: Env Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Envelope to Mix. Playing dynamics modulate the global “Chorus Mix / Vibrato / Chorale Mix” and Detune Mix amounts. Range is -100 to 100. Negative values decrease the mix levels when you attack a note, and can be used to increase the clarity of your attacks or make sustained notes more expressive over time. Playing louder will reduce the chorusing effect, and you can use large negative values to only allow chorusing when the input is soft. At zero, the envelope will not affect the chorus or detune mixes. Positive values increase the internal mix levels for chorus and detune from 0 up to the levels set by the mix knobs when you attack a note. For example, with Detune Mix set to 50, Chorus Mix set to 0, and Env Mix set to 50, when a note is attacked, the internal detune level will increase to 50, then decay towards 0 with the note. The chorus level, however, will not increase because it is at 0.

parameters.name: Tone
parameters.fullDescription: Shapes the high or low end of the output signal. Positive values roll off high frequencies Hi Cut, 0 is flat, and negative values roll off low frequencies Lo Cut. Use the Lo Cut range to reduce muddiness. Use the Hi Cut range to roll off high frequencies for a softer sound. The tone control only affects the wet signal path. Changes to the Chorus Mix knob (or usage of Env Mix) may change the apparent effect of the Tone control. For example, a setting of Hi Cut 50 will sound brighter when the Chorus Mix knob is set at 50 versus when Chorus Mix is at 100 (Vibrato).

performanceParams.name: Swirl
performanceParams.description: Adds another dimension to TriceraChorus’ sound via stereo phase shifters after the Detune section to create throbbing and swooshing effects. The amount of Swirl depends on the greater level of either Chorus Mix or Detune Mix and follows the Env Mix control. Swirl speed is adjusted by Rate and follows the ENV Rate control. Use faster rates to achieve deeper Swirl effects.

name: SpaceTime
overview: Multi-effects combining Modulation, two Delays, and Reverb into one easy-to-use effect.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Controls overall Algorithm wet/dry balance. 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Mod Amt
parameters.fullDescription: Adds Modulation to entire signal path. Modulation depth also increases as you go from 0 to 100.

parameters.name: Mod Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the speed of the LFO controlling the Modulation section of SpaceTime. Continuously adjustable from 0.05Hz to 12.50Hz.

parameters.name: Verb Lvl
parameters.description: Reverb level, series or parallel routing
parameters.fullDescription: Adjust the output level of the Reverb and routes the Reverb in Series after the Delays or Parallel with the Delays. The first half of the knob adjusts Series Reverb level from 0 to 100 while the second half of the knob switches to Parallel routing and adjusts Reverb level from 0 to 100. Percussive playing coupled with long Delay times and short Reverb Decay times will showcase parallel routing.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the decay length of the Reverb in seconds or Note Divisions when with Tempo Sync ON.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.fullDescription: Changes the Reverb character from small and dense (set to 0) to large and spacious (set to 100).

parameters.name: Delay Lvl
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of both Delays in the signal path. Can also be used to set the dry to wet blend of delayed signal sent to the Reverb in the series path. With DLY LVL set less than 50, dry signal and Delayed signal are both sent to the Reverb section. After 50, DLY LVL reduces the dry signal sent to the Reverb allowing only the delay repeats to have Reverb when the control reaches 100.

parameters.name: Delay A
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the Delay time for Delay A from 0 to 2500 ms when TEMPO Sync is OFF. With TEMPO Sync ON, Delay is sync’ed to the Tempo BPM and is adjusted in note division increments from No Delay to a Whole Note in the most common note divisions.

parameters.name: Delay B
parameters.fullDescription: See description for Delay A

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.description: Feedback amount, F1 linked / F2 independent
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the amount of feedback for both delays and contains two feedback types (F1 and F2). F1 links both delay times to create a rhythmic, repeating pattern where the longer delay sets the pattern length. The shorter delay will not repeat again until the longer delay has passed. F2 is a traditional feedback control, where delay times are independent.

name: Blackhole
overview: A galactic-scale reverb from the DSP4000 with lush sounds ideal for guitars, strings, and pads.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the relative level of the wet and dry signals.

parameters.name: Gravity
parameters.fullDescription: The Blackhole’s equivalent of decay time. On the right-hand side, the Gravity control sweeps through its forward reverb range from a very dense decay to a very long and smooth decay. On the left hand side, the Gravity control is in its inverse mode and sweeps through a range of reverse reverb-like settings.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the size of the reverb. This can range from cartoonishly small to cosmically epic.

parameters.name: Pre Delay
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the amount of delay before the reverb section. When Tempo Sync is off, this ranges from 0 ms to 2000 ms. When Tempo Sync is on, this is set in subdivisions of the tempo.

parameters.name: Low Level
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the level of low frequencies in the reverb tail using a shelving filter with a corner frequency of 350 Hz.

parameters.name: High Level
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the level of high frequencies in the reverb tail using a shelving filter with a corner frequency of 2000 Hz.

parameters.name: Mod Depth
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the modulation depth in the reverb tail. This can be a subtle control, which nevertheless can reduce ringing in the reverb tail and add some motion to the sound. This parameter is frozen while Feedback is set to Infinite or Freeze.

parameters.name: Mod Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the relative speed of the modulation in the reverb tail. Subtle but useful.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the feedback around the entire reverberation structure, for even larger sounds. Turning clockwise to Infinite will allow for infinite reverberation time, while still letting incoming signal into the reverberation structure. Turning further clockwise to Freeze sets the reverberation time to infinite, and does not allow incoming signal into the reverberation structure.

parameters.name: Resonance
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the resonance of the Low-level and High-level filters. When the filters are set to 0, this does nothing, but when they are active, it can create a much more filtered sound. But be careful, extreme settings will increase the chances of overloads.

name: DualVerb
overview: Two parallel high-quality studio reverbs with independent controls for rich stereo reverberation.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the relative level of the wet and dry signals.

parameters.name: A/B Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the input level of the A and B reverbs. When Decay is Frozen, A/B Mix is post-reverb (normally it is pre-reverb). With stereo outputs, you may turn this parameter fully clockwise for dual mono reverbs (A on left, B on right). A mono input will be sent to each reverb, while a stereo input will send input 1 to reverb A, and input 2 to reverb B.

parameters.name: Resonance
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the resonance of reverb A and B’s Tone controls. This does not affect the sound if Tone is set to 0.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the size of both reverbs A and B. Many different size combinations are possible with this one knob. The following parameters are available for both Reverb A and B:

parameters.name: Decay A
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the decay time for the reverb. When Tempo Sync is off, this ranges from 0 s to 50 s. When Tempo Sync is on, this is set in subdivisions of the tempo. Turning clockwise to Infinite will allow for infinite reverberation time, while still letting incoming signal into the reverberation structure. Turning further clockwise to Freeze sets the reverberation time to infinite, and does not allow incoming signal into the reverberation structure.

parameters.name: Decay B
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the decay time for the reverb. When Tempo Sync is off, this ranges from 0 s to 50 s. When Tempo Sync is on, this is set in subdivisions of the tempo. Turning clockwise to Infinite will allow for infinite reverberation time, while still letting incoming signal into the reverberation structure. Turning further clockwise to Freeze sets the reverberation time to infinite, and does not allow incoming signal into the reverberation structure.

parameters.name: Pre Delay A
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the amount of delay before the reverb. When Tempo Sync is off, this ranges from 0 to 900 ms. When Tempo Sync is on, this is set in subdivisions of the tempo.

parameters.name: Pre Delay B
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the amount of delay before the reverb. When Tempo Sync is off, this ranges from 0 to 900 ms. When Tempo Sync is on, this is set in subdivisions of the tempo.

parameters.name: Tone A
parameters.fullDescription: Tone control for the reverb. Negative values bring out the lows, while positive values bring out the highs.

parameters.name: Tone B
parameters.fullDescription: Tone control for the reverb. Negative values bring out the lows, while positive values bring out the highs.

name: DynaVerb
overview: Eclipse reverb coupled with Omnipressor model for dynamics-based reverb control.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Decay in seconds or note-based with Tempo Sync ON. When decay is 0, this effect can be used as a standalone Omnipressor or gate.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.fullDescription: Room size of reverb.

parameters.name: Attack Time
parameters.fullDescription: Attack time of Omnipressor/gate in seconds.

parameters.name: Low Band Shelving Level
parameters.fullDescription: Post reverb shelving boost/cut of low frequencies with cut-off at 350 Hz.

parameters.name: High Band Shelving Level
parameters.fullDescription: Post reverb shelving boost/cut of high frequencies with cutoff at 2000 Hz.

parameters.name: Compression/Expansion Ratio
parameters.fullDescription: Ratio control for the Omnipressor from traditional Gated sound, to expansion, then compression, then limiting and infinite ducking, then to negative ratios which result in dynamic reversal.

parameters.name: Release Time
parameters.fullDescription: Release time for the Omnipressor/gate in seconds.

parameters.name: Threshold
parameters.fullDescription: Threshold for the Omnipressor/gate.

parameters.name: Sidechain
parameters.fullDescription: The mixer to the sidechain input (gain control signal). When set to minimum, the gain curve is derived from the input only. At maximum, it is a feedback dynamics unit with gain derived from the reverb output. In Omnipressor Mode, this simply lets you fade between a feedforward (FF) and feedback (FB) compressor/expander/gate/etc.

name: Hall
overview: Simulates large enclosed spaces with flexible 3-band crossover reverb network.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the relative level of the wet and dry signals.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Master decay in seconds or note-based with Tempo Sync ON.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.fullDescription: Hall size.

parameters.name: Pre Delay
parameters.fullDescription: pre-delay in milliseconds or note-based with Tempo Sync ON.

parameters.name: Low Band Reverb Level
parameters.fullDescription: boost/cut of LOW reverb with cut-off at 300 Hz, -100 effectively cuts all of the low band reverb.

parameters.name: High Band Reverb Level
parameters.fullDescription: boost/cut of HIGH reverb with cut-off at 1500 Hz, -100 effectively cuts all of the high band reverb.

parameters.name: Low Band Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Decay of low band reverb, scales the Decay time.

parameters.name: High Band Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Decay of high band reverb, scales the Decay time.

parameters.name: Modulation Level
parameters.fullDescription: Increases random modulation of reverb tails.

parameters.name: Mid Band Reverb Level
parameters.fullDescription: Boost/cut of mid reverb (between 300 and 1500 Hz), -100 effectively cuts all of the mid band reverb.

name: MangledVerb
overview: Non-standard stereo reverb feeding into distortion, from subtle bow-scraping to chaotic mayhem.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mix, where 100 is an all wet signal. It has a special nonlinear taper which puts most of the knob travel in the most usable range. Note: the Mix control is not accounted for in the signal flow diagram.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Length of reverb decay scaled from 1-100. Less decay will take away reverb attack. Specifically, higher values (greater than 70) will impart traditional reverb tails, while lower values (less than 70) can result in reverse reverb sounds with more build up.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the size of the reverb. To use MangledVerb as a distortion type effect, try setting this below 15.

parameters.name: Pre Delay
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the amount of delay before the reverb section. When tempo mode is off, this range is from 0 to 1500 milliseconds. When Tempo Sync is set to ON, Pre-Delay is set in beat divisions of the tempo. The Pre-Delay control affects the Pre-Delay Block in the signal flow diagram.

parameters.name: Low Band Level
parameters.fullDescription: Boost/Cut of the low frequencies before the distortion section of the signal path.

parameters.name: Mid Band Level
parameters.fullDescription: Boost/Cut of the middle frequencies before the distortion section of the signal path.

parameters.name: High Band Level
parameters.fullDescription: Boost/Cut of the high frequencies before the distortion section of the signal path.

parameters.name: Softclip/Overdrive Type
parameters.fullDescription: Use this control to choose from two types of distortions (soft-clipping and overdrive) and set their gain/drive amount. The first half of the knob controls the Softclip gain level from 1 to 100. Passing 100 in Softclip switches the distortion type to Overdrive with a gain/drive range of 1 to 100. The gain/drive amounts in the distortion section were designed so that the transition from Softclip to Overdrive would be as smooth as possible.

parameters.name: Distortion Output Level
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the output level of the distortion section from -18 dB to +6 dB. Adding gain/drive in the distortion section via the Softclip/Overdrive control will naturally add level, so use this control accordingly. Note: The Level control knob is different than the Out Gain. The Out Gain sets the output level of the entire Algorithm (including the dry signal path).

parameters.name: Wobble
parameters.fullDescription: A modulation rate that does some spooky detuning.

name: ModEchoVerb
overview: H8000-based infinite reverb into infinite-feedback delay with modulation options.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.description: Reverb decay, Infinite sustain at max
parameters.fullDescription: Decay in seconds or note based with Tempo Sync on. All the way right Infinite gives an infinite reverb/sustain.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.fullDescription: From normal Hall type room sizes to huge canyon sounds with echoes.

parameters.name: Echo
parameters.fullDescription: Post reverb delay time in milliseconds or note-based with Tempo Sync ON.

parameters.name: Low Band Shelving Level
parameters.fullDescription: Post reverb shelving boost/cut of low frequencies with cutoff at 350 Hz.

parameters.name: High Band Shelving Level
parameters.fullDescription: Post reverb shelving boost/cut of high frequencies with cutoff at 2000 Hz.

parameters.name: Echo Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Feedback amount around the post reverb echo.

parameters.name: Modulation Rate
parameters.fullDescription: The modulation rate from 0 to 100.

parameters.name: Modulation Type & Depth
parameters.fullDescription: Select modulation type and depth: Swept Verb Flanger Mix Chorus Mix

parameters.name: Echo Tone
parameters.fullDescription: Tone control in the feedback loop of the echoes.

name: Plate
overview: Emulates early analog-mechanical reverbs with extended reverb times and extensive tone controls.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the relative level of the wet and dry signals.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.description: Reverb decay time, seconds or tempo-synced
parameters.fullDescription: Decay in seconds or note-based with Tempo Sync ON.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.fullDescription: Plate size.

parameters.name: Pre Delay
parameters.fullDescription: Pre delay in milliseconds or note-based with Tempo Sync ON.

parameters.name: Low Damp
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the damping frequency for the low end.

parameters.name: High Damp
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the damping frequency for the high end.

parameters.name: Distance
parameters.fullDescription: Sets room/transducer distance from source/plate driver.

parameters.name: Diffusion
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts diffusion amount which affects reverb build up and tail density.

parameters.name: Mod Level
parameters.fullDescription: Mixes in random modulation in reverb tail.

parameters.name: Tone
parameters.fullDescription: A pre-reverberator tone control, -100 to 0 is darker, 0 to 100 is brighter.

name: Reverse Reverb
overview: True reverse reverb followed by forward reverb with delay/feedback.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Reverse decay in milliseconds or note-based with Tempo Sync ON (also the delay amount for Late Dry Signal Level).

parameters.name: Size
parameters.fullDescription: Mixes in a standard reverb that is post reverse section for bigger sounds.

parameters.name: Feedback
parameters.fullDescription: Amount of delay feedback around reverse reverb (delay amount is Decay amount).

parameters.name: Low Level
parameters.fullDescription: Shelving boost/cut of low frequencies.

parameters.name: High Level
parameters.fullDescription: Shelving boost/cut of high frequencies.

parameters.name: Late Dry Signal Level
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts amount of dry signal that occurs directly after the reverse build up.

parameters.name: Diffusion
parameters.fullDescription: Diffusion in the reverse build-up: set to zero for a mechanical stutter.

parameters.name: Modulation Level
parameters.fullDescription: MicroPitch detuning modulation at the input.

parameters.name: Contour
parameters.fullDescription: Increase the span between low and high crossover frequencies for the Low Level and High Level. Affects the sound unless Low Level and High Level are both set to 0.

name: Room
overview: Precision room simulator from vocal booths to small halls with early/late reflection control.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Determines the relative level of the wet and dry signals.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.description: Reverb decay time, seconds or tempo-synced
parameters.fullDescription: Decay in seconds or note-based with Tempo Sync ON.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.fullDescription: Room size.

parameters.name: Pre Delay
parameters.fullDescription: Pre-delay in milliseconds or note-based with Tempo Sync ON.

parameters.name: Low Band Shelving
parameters.fullDescription: Post reverb shelving boost/cut of low frequencies with cutoff at 350 Hz.

parameters.name: High Band Shelving
parameters.fullDescription: Post reverb shelving boost/cut of high frequencies with cutoff determined by High Band Cutoff Frequency parameter.

parameters.name: Early Reflection Level
parameters.fullDescription: Control the levels of the early and late reflections.

parameters.name: Late Reflection Level
parameters.fullDescription: Control the levels of the early and late reflections.

parameters.name: Diffusion
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts diffusion amount which affects reverb build up and tail density.

parameters.name: Modulation Level
parameters.fullDescription: Adds random modulation of both diffusors and late reverb tail.

parameters.name: High Band Cutoff Frequency
parameters.fullDescription: Control the corner frequency of High Band Shelving. No affect if High Band Shelving parameter is is set to 0.

name: SP2016 Reverb
overview: Emulation of classic Eventide SP2016 Signal Processor reverb in Vintage and Modern versions.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the mix between the unprocessed input and the reverberated output. This is especially useful when some pre-delay is added.

parameters.name: Reverb Type
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the reverb Algorithm. SP2016 Reverb features three reverb Algorithms, Stereo Room, Room, and Plate. Each Algorithm is available in two versions: Vintage and Modern. The Vintage Algorithms are modeled on the original SP2016’s Algorithms and hardware, and feature a lower bit-depth than the modern versions. The Modern Algorithms are brighter, more diffuse, and use a higher bit-depth. Note The Position, Diffusion, and EQ controls are disabled for the Vintage Plate Algorithm because they were not present in the original SP2016 Algorithm.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the reverb time.

parameters.name: Position
parameters.fullDescription: This is used to move your “listening position” from the front of the “room” to the rear. You’ll find that Position is one of the most useful controls in adjusting the reverb to fit your mix. A simplified explanation: it changes the mix between the early and late reflections; what actually happens in the Algorithm is more complex than this, however.

parameters.name: Diffusion
parameters.fullDescription: This alters the character of your space – from the sharp reflections of flat, hard surfaces (low) to the diffused reflections from rough, irregular ones (high). The Diffusion control doesn’t change the decay time, but it does have an effect on the evident nature of the decay by thickening or thinning its density. Note that this can often be a subtle difference and may be difficult to hear with some types of program material and/or with long decay times. The effects of the control will be most apparent with short decays and program material with percussive attacks.

parameters.name: Pre Delay
parameters.fullDescription: Introduces a delay before the reverb effect. SP2016 Reverb is capable of long pre-delays, up to 999 milliseconds, and these can be used to create echo effects as well. Note The equalization section provides controls for high and low shelving filters. These controls affect parameters deep within the feedback structure of the reverberator and the effect may be subtle or dramatic depending on the program material and other reverb settings such as Decay, Position, or Diffusion. In general, the controls will have more pronounced effects at longer decay times and more distant position settings. Additionally, it’s usually easier to hear the effect of changes to the high frequency controls than it is to hear changes to the low frequency controls.

parameters.name: Low Freq
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the corner frequency for the low shelving filter; the range is from 50 to 500 Hz in increments of 50 Hz.

parameters.name: Low Gain
parameters.fullDescription: Adjustable gain for the low shelving filter, from -8 to +4 dB.

parameters.name: High Freq
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the corner frequency for the high shelf filter; the range is from 1000 to 8000 Hz in increments of 500 Hz.

parameters.name: High Gain
parameters.fullDescription: Adjustable gain for the high shelf filter, from -8 to 0 dB.

name: Spring
overview: Models guitar amplifier spring reverb with physical parameter access and tube-amp tremolo.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry between reverb and tremolo dry signal. If Tremolo Pre/Post is set to Pre, then this controls the wet/dry mix between the spring reverb and the tremolo’d dry signal. If Tremolo Pre/Post is set to Post, then this controls the wet/dry mix between the tremolo’d spring reverb and the unaltered dry signal.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.description: Reverb decay time, seconds or tempo-synced
parameters.fullDescription: Decay in seconds or note-based with Tempo Sync on.

parameters.name: Tension
parameters.fullDescription: Controls spring tension.

parameters.name: Number of Springs
parameters.fullDescription: Number of springs in the ‘tank,’ mixes in 1 to 3 springs.

parameters.name: Low Band Damping
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the damping frequency for the low end.

parameters.name: High Band Damping
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the damping frequency for the high end.

parameters.name: Tremolo Intensity
parameters.fullDescription: The depth of the tremolo.

parameters.name: Tremolo Rate
parameters.fullDescription: The speed of the tremolo in Hz or note-based with Tempo Sync ON.

parameters.name: Tremolo Pre/Post
parameters.fullDescription: Places the tremolo before or after the spring reverb. Note that when in Pre mode, the Mix control only affects the reverb signal. Tremolo will still be applied to the dry signal.

parameters.name: Modulation Level
parameters.fullDescription: Mixes in modulation for a nice chorusing effect.

parameters.name: Resonance
parameters.fullDescription: Metallic resonance at the High Band Damping frequency.

parameters.name: Spring Type
parameters.fullDescription: Select from 2 different reverb tank size. Large and small.

name: Shimmer
overview: Pitch-shifted reverb creating heavenly textures with two pitch shifters in feedback path.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Arbitrary 0-100 decay (less decay will also take away reverb attack).

parameters.name: Size
parameters.fullDescription: Size of the reverb.

parameters.name: Delay
parameters.fullDescription: Post reverb and pre pitch-shift delay time in milliseconds or note-based with Tempo Sync on.

parameters.name: Low Band Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Amount of post reverb and pitch-shifter low band signal (this is in the feedback path).

parameters.name: High Band Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Amount of post reverb and pitch-shifter high band signal (this is in the feedback path).

parameters.name: Pitch Shift A
parameters.fullDescription: Pitch-shifter A’s pitch in cents 500c = P4th 700c = P5th 1200c = 1 Octave 1900c = 1 Octave+P5 2400 = 2 Octaves

parameters.name: Pitch Shift B
parameters.fullDescription: See description for Pitch Shift A

parameters.name: Pitch Decay
parameters.fullDescription: The Pitch Decay knob controls the amount of pitch shifting in the reverb tail. It increases from 0 to 100. Beyond 100 are two Freeze modes. Pitch Freeze locks out the pitch shifters, but feeds the reverb, allowing you to freeze the Shimmer pitch climb at opportune times. Pitch+verb Freeze freezes everything (pitch and reverb) for dry soloing on top of the frozen reverb.

parameters.name: Mid Band Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Amount of post reverb and pitch-shifter mid band signal (this is in the feedback path).

name: TremoloVerb
overview: Large reverb controlled by aggressive tremolo with waveform, envelope, and expression pedal control.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.description: Reverb decay time, seconds or tempo-synced
parameters.fullDescription: Decay in seconds or note-based with Tempo Sync ON.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.fullDescription: Room size of reverb.

parameters.name: Pre Delay
parameters.fullDescription: Pre-delay time in milliseconds or note-based with Tempo Sync ON.

parameters.name: Low Band Shelving Level
parameters.fullDescription: Post reverb shelving boost/cut of low frequencies with cutoff at 350 Hz.

parameters.name: High Band Shelving Level
parameters.fullDescription: Post reverb shelving boost/cut of high frequencies with cutoff at High Freq.

parameters.name: Tremolo Shape
parameters.fullDescription: Selects the waveform, or source, of the modulation. The choices are: Sine Triangle Peak Random Square Ramp SampHold

parameters.name: Tremolo Speed
parameters.fullDescription: Tremolo speed in Hz, sensitivity, or note-based with Tempo Sync on.

parameters.name: Tremolo Depth
parameters.fullDescription: Tremolo depth, in stereo mode you have the option to have mono depth (same on both channels) or stereo depth (tremolo is 90 degrees out of phase).

parameters.name: High Band Cutoff Frequency
parameters.fullDescription: The high corner frequency of High-level. Affects the sound unless High-level is set to 0.

performanceParams.name: Retrigger
performanceParams.description: Retriggers the LFO to the beginning of the cycle. Useful for re-syncing during playback, or creative effects.

name: Wormhole
overview: Massive, tilting reverb with sci-fi themed controls and warping for dynamic pitch/delay modulation.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 0 is fully dry, 100 is fully wet.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.fullDescription: Wormhole distance in light years. Controls the decay time of the reverb. Internally tapered from 0 l.y. (shortest decay, 3s) to 100 l.y. (longest decay, 1000s). (0 to 100 l.y. or 30.67 parsecs).

parameters.name: Diameter
parameters.fullDescription: Width of the interstellar Wormhole. Controls the size of the reverb. A Diameter of 0 is tight enough to pull the ears off a gundark. A Diameter of 100 will fit Executor-class Super Star Destroyers eight abreast.

parameters.name: Lo Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Decay rate of the low frequency content of the reverb. 0 is quickest decay. 100 is slowest decay.

parameters.name: Hi Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Decay rate out of the high frequency content of the reverb. 0 is quickest decay. 100 is slowest decay.

parameters.name: Entry Field
parameters.fullDescription: Entrance characteristics to the Wormhole. Controls early reflections and diffusion in the reverb. 0 is a rougher entrance to the WormHole (less diffuse, noticeable early reflections). 100 is a smoother entrance to the Wormhole (more diffuse, less noticeable early reflections).

parameters.name: Pre Delay
parameters.fullDescription: Delay added before Wormhole entrance. (0 t0 2000ms)

parameters.name: Stability
parameters.fullDescription: Wormhole stability across time. Controls modulation depth of the reverb. 0% is the least stable Wormhole with large pitch variations happening to the reverb through time (max modulation/most unstable). 100% is a stable Wormhole with no reverb pitch variations through time (no modulation).

parameters.name: Stability Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Movement speed of an unstable Wormhole. Controls modulation rate of the reverb. Range is from 0 (slowest rate) to 100 (fastest modulation rate). Use in conjunction with the Stability control.

parameters.name: Warp Acceleration
parameters.fullDescription: How long it takes to traverse the wormhole and reach the top warp speed set by the Warp Factor control. Units are in total time to traverse the worm hole and lower values accelerate faster. Controls the rate of change of the reverb delay lines. (1.0 to 10s)

parameters.name: Warp Factor
parameters.fullDescription: Top warp speed desired through the worm hole. Higher Factors manifest as a higher final pitch changes. Warp Factor 10.00 theoretically highest possible speed. Controls the amount of change of the delay lines. (5 to 10s)

parameters.name: Warp Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the mix level of the effect during warping, overrides the Mix control.

parameters.name: Warp Bass
parameters.fullDescription: Bass EQ control for Warp effect.

parameters.name: Warp Treble
parameters.fullDescription: Treble EQ control for Warp effect.

performanceParams.name: Warp L
performanceParams.description: Latching footswitch. Press once to go thru the entire warping cycle. Footswitch unlatches after cycle completion.

performanceParams.name: Warp M
performanceParams.description: Momentary footswitch. Hold to keep warp cycle engaged. Releasing before cycle completion generates a “deceleration” (decreasing pitch) sound to the warp effect.

name: HotSawz
overview: Subtractive synthesis using six oscillators following a mono pitch tracker with low-pass filter and three modulation sources.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Knob has four ranges, each 0 to 100. Each range mixes the dry signal with various oscillator combinations.

parameters.name: Osc Depth
parameters.description: Adds 2nd oscillators with detuning and stereo spread
parameters.fullDescription: Mixes in 2nd oscillators for each register and adds detuning. Also spreads the oscillators across the stereo field. Modulation sources assigned to Osc Depth are additive.

parameters.name: Cutoff
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the cutoff frequency of the low pass filter. Filter is in series with wet signal.

parameters.name: Resonance
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the low pass filter’s resonance.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.description: LFO speed across four waveform ranges
parameters.fullDescription: Controls LFO’s wave shape and speed. This parameter has four ranges, each 0.1 Hz to 20 Hz or Whole note to 1/16 note divisions. Each range switches the LFO wave shape.

parameters.name: LFO Amount
parameters.fullDescription: Assigns LFO destination and controls amount of modulation. Knob has four ranges. Each range assigns the LFO to a different destination for modulation.

parameters.name: Attack
parameters.fullDescription: Gate Attack speed from 0 to 3000ms. When the Gate Sustain/Range knob is set to OFF, Attack knob has no effect.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Gate Decay speed from 0 to 3000ms. When the Gate Sustain/Range knob is set to OFF, Decay knob has no effect.

parameters.name: Gate
parameters.fullDescription: Assigns gate destination and controls amount of either Sustain or Range of the Gate. Knob has four ranges. Gate Sustain level occurs after both Attack and Decay of the Gate (There is no release in the Gate). Gate Range (for Pitch as destination) is how far from 0 pitch modulation is allowed to go at the end of Gate attack.

parameters.name: Envelope
parameters.fullDescription: This Envelope is triggered and drawn by dynamics of input level. This parameter assigns Envelope destination and controls amount of modulation. It has four ranges, each 0 to 100. Each range assigns the Envelope to a different destination for modulation.

performanceParams.name: Retrigger
performanceParams.description: Retriggers the LFO to the beginning of the cycle. Useful for re-syncing during playback, or creative effects.

name: PolySynth
overview: Polyphonic guitar-to-synth transformer using SIFT technology with multiple voices and modulation options.

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of dry and pitch shifted signal. 0 to 100.

parameters.name: Attack
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the Attack time of the generated synth sounds. 5 to 1000 ms or Tempo Based.

parameters.name: Resonance
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the resonance of the filters after each synth voice. 0 is no resonance, 100 is very resonant. 0 to 100.

parameters.name: Cutoff
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the cutoff frequency of the filter. 0 to 100.

parameters.name: Env Amount
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the maximum amount the filter cutoff will open or close to (start is always what is set by the Cutoff knob). -100 to 100.

parameters.name: Env Sense
parameters.fullDescription: Adjusts the sensitivity of the envelope or how hard do you need to play to get it to hit the value set by Env Amount. 0 to 100.

parameters.name: LFO Destination
parameters.fullDescription: Sets what parameter the LFO is modulating: Cutoff Volume Pitch PWM Detune

parameters.name: LFO Amount
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the amount of modulation the LFO applies. -100 to 100.

parameters.name: LFO Rate
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the speed of the LFO. 0.01 to 20.0 Hz or Tempo Based.

parameters.name: LFO Shape
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the shape of the LFO: Sine Triangle Square Peak Ramp Up Ramp Down Random Sample & Hold

parameters.name: Detune
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the detuning of all three voices. Adds a variable amount to each such that modulation is still heard even with two voices. -100 to 100.

parameters.name: Spread
parameters.fullDescription: Pans Voice 1 and Voice 3 to opposite sides while keeping Voice 2 in the middle. -100 to 100.

performanceParams.name: Freeze
performanceParams.description: Freezes the current synth sound, sustaining it indefinitely.

performanceParams.name: LFO Retrig
performanceParams.description: Retriggers the LFO momentarily to its starting position.

name: Synthonizer
overview: Pitch-tracking synthesizer with Voice A (additive, organ/theremin) and Voice B (subtractive, classic analog).

parameters.name: Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Wet/dry mixer, 100% is all wet signal.

parameters.name: Vox Mix
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the ratio of the two synthesized voices A and B.

parameters.name: Wave Mix A
parameters.description: Additive waveform blend for Voice A tone
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the mix of the various added waveforms to control the tone and perceived pitch of voice A.

parameters.name: Octave B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the blend between unison, 1 octave down, and 1 octave up synth voices to control the tone and perceived pitch of voice B.

parameters.name: Attack A
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the attack time for synthesized voice A.

parameters.name: Attack B
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the attack time for the filter on synthesized voice B.

parameters.name: Verb Level
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the reverb level.

parameters.name: Verb Decay
parameters.fullDescription: Sets the reverb decay time.

parameters.name: Shape A
parameters.fullDescription: Selects voice A waveshape: Sine Triangle Sawtooth Organ 1 Organ 2

parameters.name: Sweep B
parameters.description: Voice B sweepable filter, 0-50 low-pass, 50-100 high-pass
parameters.fullDescription: Controls the sweepable filter on voice B. Values from 0 to 50 sweep a low-pass filter, values from 50 to 100 sweep a high-pass filter.

performanceParams.name: Flex
performanceParams.description: Shifts both voices up one octave when activated.

name: Mute
overview: Mutes incoming audio completely.
